```````````````````````````````` example
<!-- foo -->*bar*
*baz*
.
Document scope=1:1..2:5 children=2
├── HTMLBlock scope=1:1..0:0 literal="<!-- foo -->*bar*\n" children=0
└── Paragraph scope=2:1..2:5 children=1
    └── Emphasis scope=2:1..2:5 children=1
        └── Text scope=2:2..2:4 literal="baz" children=0
````````````````````````````````


Note that anything on the last line after the
end tag will be included in the [HTML block]:

```````````````````````````````` example
<script>
foo
</script>1. *bar*
.
Document scope=1:1..3:17 children=1
└── HTMLBlock scope=1:1..2:3 literal="<script>\nfoo\n</script>1. *bar*\n" children=0
````````````````````````````````


A comment (type 2):

```````````````````````````````` example
<!-- Foo

bar
   baz -->
okay
.
Document scope=1:1..5:4 children=2
├── HTMLBlock scope=1:1..3:3 literal="<!-- Foo\n\nbar\n   baz -->\n" children=0
└── Paragraph scope=5:1..5:4 children=1
    └── Text scope=5:1..5:4 literal="okay" children=0
````````````````````````````````



A processing instruction (type 3):

```````````````````````````````` example
<?php

  echo '>';

?>
okay
.
Document scope=1:1..6:4 children=2
├── HTMLBlock scope=1:1..4:0 literal="<?php\n\n  echo '>';\n\n?>\n" children=0
└── Paragraph scope=6:1..6:4 children=1
    └── Text scope=6:1..6:4 literal="okay" children=0
````````````````````````````````


A declaration (type 4):

```````````````````````````````` example
<!DOCTYPE html>
.
Document scope=1:1..1:15 children=1
└── HTMLBlock scope=1:1..0:0 literal="<!DOCTYPE html>\n" children=0
````````````````````````````````


CDATA (type 5):

```````````````````````````````` example
<![CDATA[
function matchwo(a,b)
{
  if (a < b && a < 0) then {
    return 1;

  } else {

    return 0;
  }
}
]]>
okay
.
Document scope=1:1..13:4 children=2
├── HTMLBlock scope=1:1..11:1 literal="<![CDATA[\nfunction matchwo(a,b)\n{\n  if (a < b && a < 0) then {\n    return 1;\n\n  } else {\n\n    return 0;\n  }\n}\n]]>\n" children=0
└── Paragraph scope=13:1..13:4 children=1
    └── Text scope=13:1..13:4 literal="okay" children=0
````````````````````````````````


The opening tag can be indented 1-3 spaces, but not 4:

```````````````````````````````` example
  <!-- foo -->

    <!-- foo -->
.
Document scope=1:1..3:16 children=2
├── HTMLBlock scope=1:3..0:0 literal="  <!-- foo -->\n" children=0
└── CodeBlock scope=3:5..3:16 mode=standalone info=null language=null literal="<!-- foo -->\n" fenced=false closed=true children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
  <div>

    <div>
.
Document scope=1:1..3:9 children=2
├── HTMLBlock scope=1:3..1:7 literal="  <div>\n" children=0
└── CodeBlock scope=3:5..3:9 mode=standalone info=null language=null literal="<div>\n" fenced=false closed=true children=0
````````````````````````````````


An HTML block of types 1--6 can interrupt a paragraph, and need not be
preceded by a blank line.

```````````````````````````````` example
Foo
<div>
bar
</div>
.
Document scope=1:1..4:6 children=2
├── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:3 children=1
│   └── Text scope=1:1..1:3 literal="Foo" children=0
└── HTMLBlock scope=2:1..4:6 literal="<div>\nbar\n</div>\n" children=0
````````````````````````````````


However, a following blank line is needed, except at the end of
a document, and except for blocks of types 1--5, [above][HTML
block]:

```````````````````````````````` example
<div>
bar
</div>
*foo*
.
Document scope=1:1..4:5 children=1
└── HTMLBlock scope=1:1..4:5 literal="<div>\nbar\n</div>\n*foo*\n" children=0
````````````````````````````````


HTML blocks of type 7 cannot interrupt a paragraph:

```````````````````````````````` example
Foo
<a href="bar">
baz
.
Document scope=1:1..3:3 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..3:3 children=5
    ├── Text scope=1:1..1:3 literal="Foo" children=0
    ├── SoftBreak scope=0:0..0:0 children=0
    ├── HTML scope=2:1..2:14 literal="<a href=\"bar\">" children=0
    ├── SoftBreak scope=0:0..0:0 children=0
    └── Text scope=3:1..3:3 literal="baz" children=0
````````````````````````````````


This rule differs from John Gruber's original Markdown syntax
specification, which says:

> The only restrictions are that block-level HTML elements —
> e.g. `<div>`, `<table>`, `<pre>`, `<p>`, etc. — must be separated from
> surrounding content by blank lines, and the start and end tags of the
> block should not be indented with tabs or spaces.

In some ways Gruber's rule is more restrictive than the one given
here:

- It requires that an HTML block be preceded by a blank line.
- It does not allow the start tag to be indented.
- It requires a matching end tag, which it also does not allow to
  be indented.

Most Markdown implementations (including some of Gruber's own) do not
respect all of these restrictions.

There is one respect, however, in which Gruber's rule is more liberal
than the one given here, since it allows blank lines to occur inside
an HTML block.  There are two reasons for disallowing them here.
First, it removes the need to parse balanced tags, which is
expensive and can require backtracking from the end of the document
if no matching end tag is found. Second, it provides a very simple
and flexible way of including Markdown content inside HTML tags:
simply separate the Markdown from the HTML using blank lines:

Compare:

```````````````````````````````` example
<div>

*Emphasized* text.

</div>
.
Document scope=1:1..5:6 children=3
├── HTMLBlock scope=1:1..1:5 literal="<div>\n" children=0
├── Paragraph scope=3:1..3:18 children=2
│   ├── Emphasis scope=3:1..3:12 children=1
│   │   └── Text scope=3:2..3:11 literal="Emphasized" children=0
│   └── Text scope=3:13..3:18 literal=" text." children=0
└── HTMLBlock scope=5:1..5:6 literal="</div>\n" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
<div>
*Emphasized* text.
</div>
.
Document scope=1:1..3:6 children=1
└── HTMLBlock scope=1:1..3:6 literal="<div>\n*Emphasized* text.\n</div>\n" children=0
````````````````````````````````


Some Markdown implementations have adopted a convention of
interpreting content inside tags as text if the open tag has
the attribute `markdown=1`.  The rule given above seems a simpler and
more elegant way of achieving the same expressive power, which is also
much simpler to parse.

The main potential drawback is that one can no longer paste HTML
blocks into Markdown documents with 100% reliability.  However,
*in most cases* this will work fine, because the blank lines in
HTML are usually followed by HTML block tags.  For example:

```````````````````````````````` example
<table>

<tr>

<td>
Hi
</td>

</tr>

</table>
.
Document scope=1:1..11:8 children=5
├── HTMLBlock scope=1:1..1:7 literal="<table>\n" children=0
├── HTMLBlock scope=3:1..3:4 literal="<tr>\n" children=0
├── HTMLBlock scope=5:1..7:5 literal="<td>\nHi\n</td>\n" children=0
├── HTMLBlock scope=9:1..9:5 literal="</tr>\n" children=0
└── HTMLBlock scope=11:1..11:8 literal="</table>\n" children=0
````````````````````````````````


There are problems, however, if the inner tags are indented
*and* separated by spaces, as then they will be interpreted as
an indented code block:

```````````````````````````````` example
<table>

  <tr>

    <td>
      Hi
    </td>

  </tr>

</table>
.
Document scope=1:1..11:8 children=5
├── HTMLBlock scope=1:1..1:7 literal="<table>\n" children=0
├── HTMLBlock scope=3:3..3:6 literal="  <tr>\n" children=0
├── CodeBlock scope=5:5..8:0 mode=standalone info=null language=null literal="<td>\n  Hi\n</td>\n" fenced=false closed=true children=0
├── HTMLBlock scope=9:3..9:7 literal="  </tr>\n" children=0
└── HTMLBlock scope=11:1..11:8 literal="</table>\n" children=0
````````````````````````````````


Fortunately, blank lines are usually not necessary and can be
deleted.  The exception is inside `<pre>` tags, but as described
[above][HTML blocks], raw HTML blocks starting with `<pre>`
*can* contain blank lines.

## Link reference definitions

A [link reference definition](@)
consists of a [link label], indented up to three spaces, followed
by a colon (`:`), optional [whitespace] (including up to one
[line ending]), a [link destination],
optional [whitespace] (including up to one
[line ending]), and an optional [link
title], which if it is present must be separated
from the [link destination] by [whitespace].
No further [non-whitespace characters] may occur on the line.

A [link reference definition]
does not correspond to a structural element of a document.  Instead, it
defines a label which can be used in [reference links]
and reference-style [images] elsewhere in the document.  [Link
reference definitions] can come either before or after the links that use
them.

```````````````````````````````` example
[foo]: /url "title"

[foo]
.
Document scope=1:1..3:5 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=3:1..3:5 children=1
    └── Link scope=3:1..3:5 destination="/url" title="title" children=1
        └── Text scope=3:2..3:4 literal="foo" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
   [foo]: 
      /url  
           'the title'  

[foo]
.
Document scope=1:1..5:5 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=5:1..5:5 children=1
    └── Link scope=5:1..5:5 destination="/url" title="the title" children=1
        └── Text scope=5:2..5:4 literal="foo" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
[Foo*bar\]]:my_(url) 'title (with parens)'

[Foo*bar\]]
.
Document scope=1:1..3:11 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=3:1..3:11 children=1
    └── Link scope=3:1..3:11 destination="my_(url)" title="title (with parens)" children=1
        └── Text scope=3:2..3:10 literal="Foo*bar]" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
[Foo bar]:
<my url>
'title'

[Foo bar]
.
Document scope=1:1..5:9 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=5:1..5:9 children=1
    └── Link scope=5:1..5:9 destination="my url" title="title" children=1
        └── Text scope=5:2..5:8 literal="Foo bar" children=0
````````````````````````````````


The title may extend over multiple lines:

```````````````````````````````` example
[foo]: /url '
title
line1
line2
'

[foo]
.
Document scope=1:1..7:5 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=7:1..7:5 children=1
    └── Link scope=7:1..7:5 destination="/url" title="\ntitle\nline1\nline2\n" children=1
        └── Text scope=7:2..7:4 literal="foo" children=0
````````````````````````````````


However, it may not contain a [blank line]:

```````````````````````````````` example
[foo]: /url 'title

with blank line'

[foo]
.
Document scope=1:1..5:5 children=3
├── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:18 children=1
│   └── Text scope=1:1..1:18 literal="[foo]: /url 'title" children=0
├── Paragraph scope=3:1..3:16 children=1
│   └── Text scope=3:1..3:16 literal="with blank line'" children=0
└── Paragraph scope=5:1..5:5 children=1
    └── Text scope=5:1..5:5 literal="[foo]" children=0
````````````````````````````````


The title may be omitted:

```````````````````````````````` example
[foo]:
/url

[foo]
.
Document scope=1:1..4:5 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=4:1..4:5 children=1
    └── Link scope=4:1..4:5 destination="/url" title="" children=1
        └── Text scope=4:2..4:4 literal="foo" children=0
````````````````````````````````


The link destination may not be omitted:

```````````````````````````````` example
[foo]:

[foo]
.
Document scope=1:1..3:5 children=2
├── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:6 children=1
│   └── Text scope=1:1..1:6 literal="[foo]:" children=0
└── Paragraph scope=3:1..3:5 children=1
    └── Text scope=3:1..3:5 literal="[foo]" children=0
````````````````````````````````

 However, an empty link destination may be specified using
 angle brackets:

```````````````````````````````` example
[foo]: <>

[foo]
.
Document scope=1:1..3:5 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=3:1..3:5 children=1
    └── Link scope=3:1..3:5 destination="" title="" children=1
        └── Text scope=3:2..3:4 literal="foo" children=0
````````````````````````````````

The title must be separated from the link destination by
whitespace:

```````````````````````````````` example
[foo]: <bar>(baz)

[foo]
.
Document scope=1:1..3:5 children=2
├── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:17 children=3
│   ├── Text scope=1:1..1:7 literal="[foo]: " children=0
│   ├── HTML scope=1:8..1:12 literal="<bar>" children=0
│   └── Text scope=1:13..1:17 literal="(baz)" children=0
└── Paragraph scope=3:1..3:5 children=1
    └── Text scope=3:1..3:5 literal="[foo]" children=0
````````````````````````````````


Both title and destination can contain backslash escapes
and literal backslashes:

```````````````````````````````` example
[foo]: /url\bar\*baz "foo\"bar\baz"

[foo]
.
Document scope=1:1..3:5 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=3:1..3:5 children=1
    └── Link scope=3:1..3:5 destination="/url\\bar*baz" title="foo\"bar\\baz" children=1
        └── Text scope=3:2..3:4 literal="foo" children=0
````````````````````````````````


A link can come before its corresponding definition:

```````````````````````````````` example
[foo]

[foo]: url
.
Document scope=1:1..3:10 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:5 children=1
    └── Link scope=1:1..1:5 destination="url" title="" children=1
        └── Text scope=1:2..1:4 literal="foo" children=0
````````````````````````````````


If there are several matching definitions, the first one takes
precedence:

```````````````````````````````` example
[foo]

[foo]: first
[foo]: second
.
Document scope=1:1..4:13 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:5 children=1
    └── Link scope=1:1..1:5 destination="first" title="" children=1
        └── Text scope=1:2..1:4 literal="foo" children=0
````````````````````````````````


As noted in the section on [Links], matching of labels is
case-insensitive (see [matches]).

```````````````````````````````` example
[FOO]: /url

[Foo]
.
Document scope=1:1..3:5 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=3:1..3:5 children=1
    └── Link scope=3:1..3:5 destination="/url" title="" children=1
        └── Text scope=3:2..3:4 literal="Foo" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
[ΑΓΩ]: /φου

[αγω]
.
Document scope=1:1..3:8 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=3:1..3:8 children=1
    └── Link scope=3:1..3:8 destination="/φου" title="" children=1
        └── Text scope=3:2..3:7 literal="αγω" children=0
````````````````````````````````


Here is a link reference definition with no corresponding link.
It contributes nothing to the document.

```````````````````````````````` example
[foo]: /url
.
Document scope=1:1..1:11 children=0
````````````````````````````````


Here is another one:

```````````````````````````````` example
[
foo
]: /url
bar
.
Document scope=1:1..4:3 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..4:3 children=1
    └── Text scope=1:1..1:3 literal="bar" children=0
````````````````````````````````


This is not a link reference definition, because there are
[non-whitespace characters] after the title:

```````````````````````````````` example
[foo]: /url "title" ok
.
Document scope=1:1..1:22 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:22 children=1
    └── Text scope=1:1..1:22 literal="[foo]: /url \"title\" ok" children=0
````````````````````````````````


This is a link reference definition, but it has no title:

```````````````````````````````` example
[foo]: /url
"title" ok
.
Document scope=1:1..2:10 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..2:10 children=1
    └── Text scope=1:1..1:10 literal="\"title\" ok" children=0
````````````````````````````````


This is not a link reference definition, because it is indented
four spaces:

```````````````````````````````` example
    [foo]: /url "title"

[foo]
.
Document scope=1:1..3:5 children=2
├── CodeBlock scope=1:5..2:0 mode=standalone info=null language=null literal="[foo]: /url \"title\"\n" fenced=false closed=true children=0
└── Paragraph scope=3:1..3:5 children=1
    └── Text scope=3:1..3:5 literal="[foo]" children=0
````````````````````````````````


This is not a link reference definition, because it occurs inside
a code block:

```````````````````````````````` example
```
[foo]: /url
```

[foo]
.
Document scope=1:1..5:5 children=2
├── CodeBlock scope=1:1..3:3 mode=standalone info=null language=null literal="[foo]: /url\n" fenced=true closed=true children=0
└── Paragraph scope=5:1..5:5 children=1
    └── Text scope=5:1..5:5 literal="[foo]" children=0
````````````````````````````````


A [link reference definition] cannot interrupt a paragraph.

```````````````````````````````` example
Foo
[bar]: /baz

[bar]
.
Document scope=1:1..4:5 children=2
├── Paragraph scope=1:1..2:11 children=3
│   ├── Text scope=1:1..1:3 literal="Foo" children=0
│   ├── SoftBreak scope=0:0..0:0 children=0
│   └── Text scope=2:1..2:11 literal="[bar]: /baz" children=0
└── Paragraph scope=4:1..4:5 children=1
    └── Text scope=4:1..4:5 literal="[bar]" children=0
````````````````````````````````


However, it can directly follow other block elements, such as headings
and thematic breaks, and it need not be followed by a blank line.

```````````````````````````````` example
# [Foo]
[foo]: /url
> bar
.
Document scope=1:1..3:5 children=2
├── Heading scope=1:1..1:7 level=1 children=1
│   └── Link scope=1:3..1:7 destination="/url" title="" children=1
│       └── Text scope=1:4..1:6 literal="Foo" children=0
└── BlockQuote scope=3:1..3:5 children=1
    └── Paragraph scope=3:3..3:5 children=1
        └── Text scope=3:3..3:5 literal="bar" children=0
````````````````````````````````

```````````````````````````````` example
[foo]: /url
bar
===
[foo]
.
Document scope=1:1..4:5 children=2
├── Heading scope=1:1..4:5 level=1 children=1
│   └── Text scope=1:1..1:3 literal="bar" children=0
└── Paragraph scope=4:1..4:5 children=1
    └── Link scope=4:1..4:5 destination="/url" title="" children=1
        └── Text scope=4:2..4:4 literal="foo" children=0
````````````````````````````````

```````````````````````````````` example
[foo]: /url
===
[foo]
.
Document scope=1:1..3:5 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..3:5 children=3
    ├── Text scope=1:1..1:3 literal="===" children=0
    ├── SoftBreak scope=0:0..0:0 children=0
    └── Link scope=2:1..2:5 destination="/url" title="" children=1
        └── Text scope=2:2..2:4 literal="foo" children=0
````````````````````````````````


Several [link reference definitions]
can occur one after another, without intervening blank lines.

```````````````````````````````` example
[foo]: /foo-url "foo"
[bar]: /bar-url
  "bar"
[baz]: /baz-url

[foo],
[bar],
[baz]
.
Document scope=1:1..8:5 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=6:1..8:5 children=7
    ├── Link scope=6:1..6:5 destination="/foo-url" title="foo" children=1
    │   └── Text scope=6:2..6:4 literal="foo" children=0
    ├── Text scope=6:6..6:6 literal="," children=0
    ├── SoftBreak scope=0:0..0:0 children=0
    ├── Link scope=7:1..7:5 destination="/bar-url" title="bar" children=1
    │   └── Text scope=7:2..7:4 literal="bar" children=0
    ├── Text scope=7:6..7:6 literal="," children=0
    ├── SoftBreak scope=0:0..0:0 children=0
    └── Link scope=8:1..8:5 destination="/baz-url" title="" children=1
        └── Text scope=8:2..8:4 literal="baz" children=0
````````````````````````````````


[Link reference definitions] can occur
inside block containers, like lists and block quotations.  They
affect the entire document, not just the container in which they
are defined:

```````````````````````````````` example
[foo]

> [foo]: /url
.
Document scope=1:1..3:13 children=2
├── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:5 children=1
│   └── Link scope=1:1..1:5 destination="/url" title="" children=1
│       └── Text scope=1:2..1:4 literal="foo" children=0
└── BlockQuote scope=3:1..3:13 children=0
````````````````````````````````


Whether something is a [link reference definition] is
independent of whether the link reference it defines is
used in the document.  Thus, for example, the following
document contains just a link reference definition, and
no visible content:

```````````````````````````````` example
[foo]: /url
.
Document scope=1:1..1:11 children=0
````````````````````````````````


## Paragraphs

A sequence of non-blank lines that cannot be interpreted as other
kinds of blocks forms a [paragraph](@).
The contents of the paragraph are the result of parsing the
paragraph's raw content as inlines.  The paragraph's raw content
is formed by concatenating the lines and removing initial and final
[whitespace].

A simple example with two paragraphs:

```````````````````````````````` example
aaa

bbb
.
Document scope=1:1..3:3 children=2
├── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:3 children=1
│   └── Text scope=1:1..1:3 literal="aaa" children=0
└── Paragraph scope=3:1..3:3 children=1
    └── Text scope=3:1..3:3 literal="bbb" children=0
````````````````````````````````


Paragraphs can contain multiple lines, but no blank lines:

```````````````````````````````` example
aaa
bbb

ccc
ddd
.
Document scope=1:1..5:3 children=2
├── Paragraph scope=1:1..2:3 children=3
│   ├── Text scope=1:1..1:3 literal="aaa" children=0
│   ├── SoftBreak scope=0:0..0:0 children=0
│   └── Text scope=2:1..2:3 literal="bbb" children=0
└── Paragraph scope=4:1..5:3 children=3
    ├── Text scope=4:1..4:3 literal="ccc" children=0
    ├── SoftBreak scope=0:0..0:0 children=0
    └── Text scope=5:1..5:3 literal="ddd" children=0
````````````````````````````````


Multiple blank lines between paragraph have no effect:

```````````````````````````````` example
aaa


bbb
.
Document scope=1:1..4:3 children=2
├── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:3 children=1
│   └── Text scope=1:1..1:3 literal="aaa" children=0
└── Paragraph scope=4:1..4:3 children=1
    └── Text scope=4:1..4:3 literal="bbb" children=0
````````````````````````````````


Leading spaces are skipped:

```````````````````````````````` example
  aaa
 bbb
.
Document scope=1:1..2:4 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:3..2:4 children=3
    ├── Text scope=1:3..1:5 literal="aaa" children=0
    ├── SoftBreak scope=0:0..0:0 children=0
    └── Text scope=2:3..2:5 literal="bbb" children=0
````````````````````````````````


Lines after the first may be indented any amount, since indented
code blocks cannot interrupt paragraphs.

```````````````````````````````` example
aaa
             bbb
                                       ccc
.
Document scope=1:1..3:42 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..3:42 children=5
    ├── Text scope=1:1..1:3 literal="aaa" children=0
    ├── SoftBreak scope=0:0..0:0 children=0
    ├── Text scope=2:1..2:3 literal="bbb" children=0
    ├── SoftBreak scope=0:0..0:0 children=0
    └── Text scope=3:1..3:3 literal="ccc" children=0
````````````````````````````````


However, the first line may be indented at most three spaces,
or an indented code block will be triggered:

```````````````````````````````` example
   aaa
bbb
.
Document scope=1:1..2:3 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:4..2:3 children=3
    ├── Text scope=1:4..1:6 literal="aaa" children=0
    ├── SoftBreak scope=0:0..0:0 children=0
    └── Text scope=2:4..2:6 literal="bbb" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
    aaa
bbb
.
Document scope=1:1..2:3 children=2
├── CodeBlock scope=1:5..1:7 mode=standalone info=null language=null literal="aaa\n" fenced=false closed=true children=0
└── Paragraph scope=2:1..2:3 children=1
    └── Text scope=2:1..2:3 literal="bbb" children=0
````````````````````````````````


Final spaces are stripped before inline parsing, so a paragraph
that ends with two or more spaces will not end with a [hard line
break]:

```````````````````````````````` example
aaa     
bbb     
.
Document scope=1:1..2:8 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..2:8 children=3
    ├── Text scope=1:1..1:8 literal="aaa" children=0
    ├── LineBreak scope=0:0..0:0 children=0
    └── Text scope=2:1..2:3 literal="bbb" children=0
````````````````````````````````


## Blank lines

[Blank lines] between block-level elements are ignored,
except for the role they play in determining whether a [list]
is [tight] or [loose].

Blank lines at the beginning and end of the document are also ignored.

```````````````````````````````` example
  

aaa
  

# aaa

  
.
Document scope=1:1..8:2 children=2
├── Paragraph scope=3:1..3:3 children=1
│   └── Text scope=3:1..3:3 literal="aaa" children=0
└── Heading scope=6:1..6:5 level=1 children=1
    └── Text scope=6:3..6:5 literal="aaa" children=0
````````````````````````````````

<div class="extension">

## Tables (extension)

GFM enables the `table` extension, where an additional leaf block type is
available.

A [table](@) is an arrangement of data with rows and columns, consisting of a
single header row, a [delimiter row] separating the header from the data, and
zero or more data rows.

Each row consists of cells containing arbitrary text, in which [inlines] are
parsed, separated by pipes (`|`).  A leading and trailing pipe is also
recommended for clarity of reading, and if there's otherwise parsing ambiguity.
Spaces between pipes and cell content are trimmed.  Block-level elements cannot
be inserted in a table.

The [delimiter row](@) consists of cells whose only content are hyphens (`-`),
and optionally, a leading or trailing colon (`:`), or both, to indicate left,
right, or center alignment respectively.

```````````````````````````````` example table
| foo | bar |
| --- | --- |
| baz | bim |
.
Document scope=1:1..3:13 children=1
└── Table scope=1:1..3:13 alignments=[none,none] children=2
    ├── TableRow scope=1:1..1:13 isHeader=true children=2
    │   ├── TableCell scope=1:2..1:6 children=1
    │   │   └── Text scope=1:3..1:5 literal="foo" children=0
    │   └── TableCell scope=1:8..1:12 children=1
    │       └── Text scope=1:9..1:11 literal="bar" children=0
    └── TableRow scope=3:1..3:13 isHeader=false children=2
        ├── TableCell scope=3:2..3:6 children=1
        │   └── Text scope=3:3..3:5 literal="baz" children=0
        └── TableCell scope=3:8..3:12 children=1
            └── Text scope=3:9..3:11 literal="bim" children=0
````````````````````````````````

Cells in one column don't need to match length, though it's easier to read if
they are. Likewise, use of leading and trailing pipes may be inconsistent:

```````````````````````````````` example table
| abc | defghi |
:-: | -----------:
bar | baz
.
Document scope=1:1..3:9 children=1
└── Table scope=1:1..3:9 alignments=[center,right] children=2
    ├── TableRow scope=1:1..1:16 isHeader=true children=2
    │   ├── TableCell scope=1:2..1:6 children=1
    │   │   └── Text scope=1:3..1:5 literal="abc" children=0
    │   └── TableCell scope=1:8..1:15 children=1
    │       └── Text scope=1:9..1:14 literal="defghi" children=0
    └── TableRow scope=3:1..3:9 isHeader=false children=2
        ├── TableCell scope=3:1..3:4 children=1
        │   └── Text scope=3:1..3:3 literal="bar" children=0
        └── TableCell scope=3:6..3:9 children=1
            └── Text scope=3:7..3:9 literal="baz" children=0
````````````````````````````````

Include a pipe in a cell's content by escaping it, including inside other
inline spans:

```````````````````````````````` example table
| f\|oo  |
| ------ |
| b `\|` az |
| b **\|** im |
.
Document scope=1:1..4:15 children=1
└── Table scope=1:1..4:15 alignments=[none] children=3
    ├── TableRow scope=1:1..1:10 isHeader=true children=1
    │   └── TableCell scope=1:2..1:9 children=1
    │       └── Text scope=1:3..1:6 literal="f|oo" children=0
    ├── TableRow scope=3:1..3:13 isHeader=false children=1
    │   └── TableCell scope=3:2..3:12 children=3
    │       ├── Text scope=3:3..3:4 literal="b " children=0
    │       ├── Code scope=3:6..3:6 mode=embedded literal="|" children=0
    │       └── Text scope=3:8..3:10 literal=" az" children=0
    └── TableRow scope=4:1..4:15 isHeader=false children=1
        └── TableCell scope=4:2..4:14 children=3
            ├── Text scope=4:3..4:4 literal="b " children=0
            ├── Strong scope=4:5..4:9 children=1
            │   └── Text scope=4:7..4:7 literal="|" children=0
            └── Text scope=4:10..4:12 literal=" im" children=0
````````````````````````````````

The table is broken at the first empty line, or beginning of another
block-level structure:

```````````````````````````````` example table
| abc | def |
| --- | --- |
| bar | baz |
> bar
.
Document scope=1:1..4:5 children=2
├── Table scope=1:1..3:13 alignments=[none,none] children=2
│   ├── TableRow scope=1:1..1:13 isHeader=true children=2
│   │   ├── TableCell scope=1:2..1:6 children=1
│   │   │   └── Text scope=1:3..1:5 literal="abc" children=0
│   │   └── TableCell scope=1:8..1:12 children=1
│   │       └── Text scope=1:9..1:11 literal="def" children=0
│   └── TableRow scope=3:1..3:13 isHeader=false children=2
│       ├── TableCell scope=3:2..3:6 children=1
│       │   └── Text scope=3:3..3:5 literal="bar" children=0
│       └── TableCell scope=3:8..3:12 children=1
│           └── Text scope=3:9..3:11 literal="baz" children=0
└── BlockQuote scope=4:1..4:5 children=1
    └── Paragraph scope=4:3..4:5 children=1
        └── Text scope=4:3..4:5 literal="bar" children=0
````````````````````````````````

```````````````````````````````` example table
| abc | def |
| --- | --- |
| bar | baz |
bar

bar
.
Document scope=1:1..6:3 children=2
├── Table scope=1:1..4:3 alignments=[none,none] children=3
│   ├── TableRow scope=1:1..1:13 isHeader=true children=2
│   │   ├── TableCell scope=1:2..1:6 children=1
│   │   │   └── Text scope=1:3..1:5 literal="abc" children=0
│   │   └── TableCell scope=1:8..1:12 children=1
│   │       └── Text scope=1:9..1:11 literal="def" children=0
│   ├── TableRow scope=3:1..3:13 isHeader=false children=2
│   │   ├── TableCell scope=3:2..3:6 children=1
│   │   │   └── Text scope=3:3..3:5 literal="bar" children=0
│   │   └── TableCell scope=3:8..3:12 children=1
│   │       └── Text scope=3:9..3:11 literal="baz" children=0
│   └── TableRow scope=4:1..4:3 isHeader=false children=2
│       ├── TableCell scope=4:1..4:3 children=1
│       │   └── Text scope=4:1..4:3 literal="bar" children=0
│       └── TableCell scope=4:0..4:0 children=0
└── Paragraph scope=6:1..6:3 children=1
    └── Text scope=6:1..6:3 literal="bar" children=0
````````````````````````````````

The header row must match the [delimiter row] in the number of cells.  If not,
a table will not be recognized:

```````````````````````````````` example table
| abc | def |
| --- |
| bar |
.
Document scope=1:1..3:7 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..3:7 children=5
    ├── Text scope=1:1..1:13 literal="| abc | def |" children=0
    ├── SoftBreak scope=0:0..0:0 children=0
    ├── Text scope=2:1..2:7 literal="| --- |" children=0
    ├── SoftBreak scope=0:0..0:0 children=0
    └── Text scope=3:1..3:7 literal="| bar |" children=0
````````````````````````````````

The remainder of the table's rows may vary in the number of cells.  If there
are a number of cells fewer than the number of cells in the header row, empty
cells are inserted.  If there are greater, the excess is ignored:

```````````````````````````````` example table
| abc | def |
| --- | --- |
| bar |
| bar | baz | boo |
.
Document scope=1:1..4:19 children=1
└── Table scope=1:1..4:19 alignments=[none,none] children=3
    ├── TableRow scope=1:1..1:13 isHeader=true children=2
    │   ├── TableCell scope=1:2..1:6 children=1
    │   │   └── Text scope=1:3..1:5 literal="abc" children=0
    │   └── TableCell scope=1:8..1:12 children=1
    │       └── Text scope=1:9..1:11 literal="def" children=0
    ├── TableRow scope=3:1..3:7 isHeader=false children=2
    │   ├── TableCell scope=3:2..3:6 children=1
    │   │   └── Text scope=3:3..3:5 literal="bar" children=0
    │   └── TableCell scope=3:0..3:0 children=0
    └── TableRow scope=4:1..4:19 isHeader=false children=2
        ├── TableCell scope=4:2..4:6 children=1
        │   └── Text scope=4:3..4:5 literal="bar" children=0
        └── TableCell scope=4:8..4:12 children=1
            └── Text scope=4:9..4:11 literal="baz" children=0
````````````````````````````````

If there are no rows in the body, no `<tbody>` is generated in HTML output:

```````````````````````````````` example table
| abc | def |
| --- | --- |
.
Document scope=1:1..2:13 children=1
└── Table scope=1:1..2:13 alignments=[none,none] children=1
    └── TableRow scope=1:1..1:13 isHeader=true children=2
        ├── TableCell scope=1:2..1:6 children=1
        │   └── Text scope=1:3..1:5 literal="abc" children=0
        └── TableCell scope=1:8..1:12 children=1
            └── Text scope=1:9..1:11 literal="def" children=0
````````````````````````````````

</div>

# Container blocks

A [container block](#container-blocks) is a block that has other
blocks as its contents.  There are two basic kinds of container blocks:
[block quotes] and [list items].
[Lists] are meta-containers for [list items].

We define the syntax for container blocks recursively.  The general
form of the definition is:

> If X is a sequence of blocks, then the result of
> transforming X in such-and-such a way is a container of type Y
> with these blocks as its content.

So, we explain what counts as a block quote or list item by explaining
how these can be *generated* from their contents. This should suffice
to define the syntax, although it does not give a recipe for *parsing*
these constructions.  (A recipe is provided below in the section entitled
[A parsing strategy](#appendix-a-parsing-strategy).)

## Block quotes

A [block quote marker](@)
consists of 0-3 spaces of initial indent, plus (a) the character `>` together
with a following space, or (b) a single character `>` not followed by a space.

The following rules define [block quotes]:

1.  **Basic case.**  If a string of lines *Ls* constitute a sequence
    of blocks *Bs*, then the result of prepending a [block quote
    marker] to the beginning of each line in *Ls*
    is a [block quote](#block-quotes) containing *Bs*.

2.  **Laziness.**  If a string of lines *Ls* constitute a [block
    quote](#block-quotes) with contents *Bs*, then the result of deleting
    the initial [block quote marker] from one or
    more lines in which the next [non-whitespace character] after the [block
    quote marker] is [paragraph continuation
    text] is a block quote with *Bs* as its content.
    [Paragraph continuation text](@) is text
    that will be parsed as part of the content of a paragraph, but does
    not occur at the beginning of the paragraph.

3.  **Consecutiveness.**  A document cannot contain two [block
    quotes] in a row unless there is a [blank line] between them.

Nothing else counts as a [block quote](#block-quotes).

Here is a simple example:

```````````````````````````````` example
> # Foo
> bar
> baz
.
Document scope=1:1..3:5 children=1
└── BlockQuote scope=1:1..3:5 children=2
    ├── Heading scope=1:3..1:7 level=1 children=1
    │   └── Text scope=1:5..1:7 literal="Foo" children=0
    └── Paragraph scope=2:3..3:5 children=3
        ├── Text scope=2:3..2:5 literal="bar" children=0
        ├── SoftBreak scope=0:0..0:0 children=0
        └── Text scope=3:3..3:5 literal="baz" children=0
````````````````````````````````


The spaces after the `>` characters can be omitted:

```````````````````````````````` example
># Foo
>bar
> baz
.
Document scope=1:1..3:5 children=1
└── BlockQuote scope=1:1..3:5 children=2
    ├── Heading scope=1:2..1:6 level=1 children=1
    │   └── Text scope=1:4..1:6 literal="Foo" children=0
    └── Paragraph scope=2:2..3:5 children=3
        ├── Text scope=2:2..2:4 literal="bar" children=0
        ├── SoftBreak scope=0:0..0:0 children=0
        └── Text scope=3:2..3:4 literal="baz" children=0
````````````````````````````````


The `>` characters can be indented 1-3 spaces:

```````````````````````````````` example
   > # Foo
   > bar
 > baz
.
Document scope=1:1..3:6 children=1
└── BlockQuote scope=1:4..3:6 children=2
    ├── Heading scope=1:6..1:10 level=1 children=1
    │   └── Text scope=1:8..1:10 literal="Foo" children=0
    └── Paragraph scope=2:6..3:6 children=3
        ├── Text scope=2:6..2:8 literal="bar" children=0
        ├── SoftBreak scope=0:0..0:0 children=0
        └── Text scope=3:6..3:8 literal="baz" children=0
````````````````````````````````


Four spaces gives us a code block:

```````````````````````````````` example
    > # Foo
    > bar
    > baz
.
Document scope=1:1..3:9 children=1
└── CodeBlock scope=1:5..3:9 mode=standalone info=null language=null literal="> # Foo\n> bar\n> baz\n" fenced=false closed=true children=0
````````````````````````````````


The Laziness clause allows us to omit the `>` before
[paragraph continuation text]:

```````````````````````````````` example
> # Foo
> bar
baz
.
Document scope=1:1..3:3 children=1
└── BlockQuote scope=1:1..3:3 children=2
    ├── Heading scope=1:3..1:7 level=1 children=1
    │   └── Text scope=1:5..1:7 literal="Foo" children=0
    └── Paragraph scope=2:3..3:3 children=3
        ├── Text scope=2:3..2:5 literal="bar" children=0
        ├── SoftBreak scope=0:0..0:0 children=0
        └── Text scope=3:3..3:5 literal="baz" children=0
````````````````````````````````


A block quote can contain some lazy and some non-lazy
continuation lines:

```````````````````````````````` example
> bar
baz
> foo
.
Document scope=1:1..3:5 children=1
└── BlockQuote scope=1:1..3:5 children=1
    └── Paragraph scope=1:3..3:5 children=5
        ├── Text scope=1:3..1:5 literal="bar" children=0
        ├── SoftBreak scope=0:0..0:0 children=0
        ├── Text scope=2:3..2:5 literal="baz" children=0
        ├── SoftBreak scope=0:0..0:0 children=0
        └── Text scope=3:3..3:5 literal="foo" children=0
````````````````````````````````


Laziness only applies to lines that would have been continuations of
paragraphs had they been prepended with [block quote markers].
For example, the `> ` cannot be omitted in the second line of

``` markdown
> foo
> ---
```

without changing the meaning:

```````````````````````````````` example
> foo
---
.
Document scope=1:1..2:3 children=2
├── BlockQuote scope=1:1..1:5 children=1
│   └── Paragraph scope=1:3..1:5 children=1
│       └── Text scope=1:3..1:5 literal="foo" children=0
└── ThematicBreak scope=2:1..2:3 children=0
````````````````````````````````


Similarly, if we omit the `> ` in the second line of

``` markdown
> - foo
> - bar
```

then the block quote ends after the first line:

```````````````````````````````` example
> - foo
- bar
.
Document scope=1:1..2:5 children=2
├── BlockQuote scope=1:1..1:7 children=1
│   └── List scope=1:3..1:7 flavor=bullet start=null tight=true children=1
│       └── ListItem scope=1:3..1:7 checked=null children=1
│           └── Paragraph scope=1:5..1:7 children=1
│               └── Text scope=1:5..1:7 literal="foo" children=0
└── List scope=2:1..2:5 flavor=bullet start=null tight=true children=1
    └── ListItem scope=2:1..2:5 checked=null children=1
        └── Paragraph scope=2:3..2:5 children=1
            └── Text scope=2:3..2:5 literal="bar" children=0
````````````````````````````````


For the same reason, we can't omit the `> ` in front of
subsequent lines of an indented or fenced code block:

```````````````````````````````` example
>     foo
    bar
.
Document scope=1:1..2:7 children=2
├── BlockQuote scope=1:1..1:9 children=1
│   └── CodeBlock scope=1:7..1:9 mode=standalone info=null language=null literal="foo\n" fenced=false closed=true children=0
└── CodeBlock scope=2:5..2:7 mode=standalone info=null language=null literal="bar\n" fenced=false closed=true children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
> ```
foo
```
.
Document scope=1:1..3:3 children=3
├── BlockQuote scope=1:1..1:5 children=1
│   └── CodeBlock scope=1:3..2:3 mode=standalone info=null language=null literal="" fenced=true closed=false children=0
├── Paragraph scope=2:1..2:3 children=1
│   └── Text scope=2:1..2:3 literal="foo" children=0
└── CodeBlock scope=3:1..3:3 mode=standalone info=null language=null literal="" fenced=true closed=false children=0
````````````````````````````````


Note that in the following case, we have a [lazy
continuation line]:

```````````````````````````````` example
> foo
    - bar
.
Document scope=1:1..2:9 children=1
└── BlockQuote scope=1:1..2:9 children=1
    └── Paragraph scope=1:3..2:9 children=3
        ├── Text scope=1:3..1:5 literal="foo" children=0
        ├── SoftBreak scope=0:0..0:0 children=0
        └── Text scope=2:7..2:11 literal="- bar" children=0
````````````````````````````````


To see why, note that in

```markdown
> foo
>     - bar
```

the `- bar` is indented too far to start a list, and can't
be an indented code block because indented code blocks cannot
interrupt paragraphs, so it is [paragraph continuation text].

A block quote can be empty:

```````````````````````````````` example
>
.
Document scope=1:1..1:1 children=1
└── BlockQuote scope=1:1..1:1 children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
>
>  
> 
.
Document scope=1:1..3:2 children=1
└── BlockQuote scope=1:1..3:2 children=0
````````````````````````````````


A block quote can have initial or final blank lines:

```````````````````````````````` example
>
> foo
>  
.
Document scope=1:1..3:3 children=1
└── BlockQuote scope=1:1..3:3 children=1
    └── Paragraph scope=2:3..2:5 children=1
        └── Text scope=2:3..2:5 literal="foo" children=0
````````````````````````````````


A blank line always separates block quotes:

```````````````````````````````` example
> foo

> bar
.
Document scope=1:1..3:5 children=2
├── BlockQuote scope=1:1..1:5 children=1
│   └── Paragraph scope=1:3..1:5 children=1
│       └── Text scope=1:3..1:5 literal="foo" children=0
└── BlockQuote scope=3:1..3:5 children=1
    └── Paragraph scope=3:3..3:5 children=1
        └── Text scope=3:3..3:5 literal="bar" children=0
````````````````````````````````


(Most current Markdown implementations, including John Gruber's
original `Markdown.pl`, will parse this example as a single block quote
with two paragraphs.  But it seems better to allow the author to decide
whether two block quotes or one are wanted.)

Consecutiveness means that if we put these block quotes together,
we get a single block quote:

```````````````````````````````` example
> foo
> bar
.
Document scope=1:1..2:5 children=1
└── BlockQuote scope=1:1..2:5 children=1
    └── Paragraph scope=1:3..2:5 children=3
        ├── Text scope=1:3..1:5 literal="foo" children=0
        ├── SoftBreak scope=0:0..0:0 children=0
        └── Text scope=2:3..2:5 literal="bar" children=0
````````````````````````````````


To get a block quote with two paragraphs, use:

```````````````````````````````` example
> foo
>
> bar
.
Document scope=1:1..3:5 children=1
└── BlockQuote scope=1:1..3:5 children=2
    ├── Paragraph scope=1:3..1:5 children=1
    │   └── Text scope=1:3..1:5 literal="foo" children=0
    └── Paragraph scope=3:3..3:5 children=1
        └── Text scope=3:3..3:5 literal="bar" children=0
````````````````````````````````


Block quotes can interrupt paragraphs:

```````````````````````````````` example
foo
> bar
.
Document scope=1:1..2:5 children=2
├── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:3 children=1
│   └── Text scope=1:1..1:3 literal="foo" children=0
└── BlockQuote scope=2:1..2:5 children=1
    └── Paragraph scope=2:3..2:5 children=1
        └── Text scope=2:3..2:5 literal="bar" children=0
````````````````````````````````


In general, blank lines are not needed before or after block
quotes:

```````````````````````````````` example
> aaa
***
> bbb
.
Document scope=1:1..3:5 children=3
├── BlockQuote scope=1:1..1:5 children=1
│   └── Paragraph scope=1:3..1:5 children=1
│       └── Text scope=1:3..1:5 literal="aaa" children=0
├── ThematicBreak scope=2:1..2:3 children=0
└── BlockQuote scope=3:1..3:5 children=1
    └── Paragraph scope=3:3..3:5 children=1
        └── Text scope=3:3..3:5 literal="bbb" children=0
````````````````````````````````


However, because of laziness, a blank line is needed between
a block quote and a following paragraph:

```````````````````````````````` example
> bar
baz
.
Document scope=1:1..2:3 children=1
└── BlockQuote scope=1:1..2:3 children=1
    └── Paragraph scope=1:3..2:3 children=3
        ├── Text scope=1:3..1:5 literal="bar" children=0
        ├── SoftBreak scope=0:0..0:0 children=0
        └── Text scope=2:3..2:5 literal="baz" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
> bar

baz
.
Document scope=1:1..3:3 children=2
├── BlockQuote scope=1:1..1:5 children=1
│   └── Paragraph scope=1:3..1:5 children=1
│       └── Text scope=1:3..1:5 literal="bar" children=0
└── Paragraph scope=3:1..3:3 children=1
    └── Text scope=3:1..3:3 literal="baz" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
> bar
>
baz
.
Document scope=1:1..3:3 children=2
├── BlockQuote scope=1:1..2:1 children=1
│   └── Paragraph scope=1:3..1:5 children=1
│       └── Text scope=1:3..1:5 literal="bar" children=0
└── Paragraph scope=3:1..3:3 children=1
    └── Text scope=3:1..3:3 literal="baz" children=0
````````````````````````````````


It is a consequence of the Laziness rule that any number
of initial `>`s may be omitted on a continuation line of a
nested block quote:

```````````````````````````````` example
> > > foo
bar
.
Document scope=1:1..2:3 children=1
└── BlockQuote scope=1:1..2:3 children=1
    └── BlockQuote scope=1:3..2:3 children=1
        └── BlockQuote scope=1:5..2:3 children=1
            └── Paragraph scope=1:7..2:3 children=3
                ├── Text scope=1:7..1:9 literal="foo" children=0
                ├── SoftBreak scope=0:0..0:0 children=0
                └── Text scope=2:7..2:9 literal="bar" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
>>> foo
> bar
>>baz
.
Document scope=1:1..3:5 children=1
└── BlockQuote scope=1:1..3:5 children=1
    └── BlockQuote scope=1:2..3:5 children=1
        └── BlockQuote scope=1:3..3:5 children=1
            └── Paragraph scope=1:5..3:5 children=5
                ├── Text scope=1:5..1:7 literal="foo" children=0
                ├── SoftBreak scope=0:0..0:0 children=0
                ├── Text scope=2:5..2:7 literal="bar" children=0
                ├── SoftBreak scope=0:0..0:0 children=0
                └── Text scope=3:5..3:7 literal="baz" children=0
````````````````````````````````


When including an indented code block in a block quote,
remember that the [block quote marker] includes
both the `>` and a following space.  So *five spaces* are needed after
the `>`:

```````````````````````````````` example
>     code

>    not code
.
Document scope=1:1..3:13 children=2
├── BlockQuote scope=1:1..1:10 children=1
│   └── CodeBlock scope=1:7..1:10 mode=standalone info=null language=null literal="code\n" fenced=false closed=true children=0
└── BlockQuote scope=3:1..3:13 children=1
    └── Paragraph scope=3:6..3:13 children=1
        └── Text scope=3:6..3:13 literal="not code" children=0
````````````````````````````````



## List items

A [list marker](@) is a
[bullet list marker] or an [ordered list marker].

A [bullet list marker](@)
is a `-`, `+`, or `*` character.

An [ordered list marker](@)
is a sequence of 1--9 arabic digits (`0-9`), followed by either a
`.` character or a `)` character.  (The reason for the length
limit is that with 10 digits we start seeing integer overflows
in some browsers.)

The following rules define [list items]:

1.  **Basic case.**  If a sequence of lines *Ls* constitute a sequence of
    blocks *Bs* starting with a [non-whitespace character], and *M* is a
    list marker of width *W* followed by 1 ≤ *N* ≤ 4 spaces, then the result
    of prepending *M* and the following spaces to the first line of
    *Ls*, and indenting subsequent lines of *Ls* by *W + N* spaces, is a
    list item with *Bs* as its contents.  The type of the list item
    (bullet or ordered) is determined by the type of its list marker.
    If the list item is ordered, then it is also assigned a start
    number, based on the ordered list marker.

    Exceptions:

    1. When the first list item in a [list] interrupts
    a paragraph---that is, when it starts on a line that would
    otherwise count as [paragraph continuation text]---then (a)
    the lines *Ls* must not begin with a blank line, and (b) if
    the list item is ordered, the start number must be 1.
    2. If any line is a [thematic break][thematic breaks] then
       that line is not a list item.

For example, let *Ls* be the lines

```````````````````````````````` example
A paragraph
with two lines.

    indented code

> A block quote.
.
Document scope=1:1..6:16 children=3
├── Paragraph scope=1:1..2:15 children=3
│   ├── Text scope=1:1..1:11 literal="A paragraph" children=0
│   ├── SoftBreak scope=0:0..0:0 children=0
│   └── Text scope=2:1..2:15 literal="with two lines." children=0
├── CodeBlock scope=4:5..5:0 mode=standalone info=null language=null literal="indented code\n" fenced=false closed=true children=0
└── BlockQuote scope=6:1..6:16 children=1
    └── Paragraph scope=6:3..6:16 children=1
        └── Text scope=6:3..6:16 literal="A block quote." children=0
````````````````````````````````


And let *M* be the marker `1.`, and *N* = 2.  Then rule #1 says
that the following is an ordered list item with start number 1,
and the same contents as *Ls*:

```````````````````````````````` example
1.  A paragraph
    with two lines.

        indented code

    > A block quote.
.
Document scope=1:1..6:20 children=1
└── List scope=1:1..6:20 flavor=ordered start=1 tight=false children=1
    └── ListItem scope=1:1..6:20 checked=null children=3
        ├── Paragraph scope=1:5..2:19 children=3
        │   ├── Text scope=1:5..1:15 literal="A paragraph" children=0
        │   ├── SoftBreak scope=0:0..0:0 children=0
        │   └── Text scope=2:5..2:19 literal="with two lines." children=0
        ├── CodeBlock scope=4:9..5:0 mode=standalone info=null language=null literal="indented code\n" fenced=false closed=true children=0
        └── BlockQuote scope=6:5..6:20 children=1
            └── Paragraph scope=6:7..6:20 children=1
                └── Text scope=6:7..6:20 literal="A block quote." children=0
````````````````````````````````


The most important thing to notice is that the position of
the text after the list marker determines how much indentation
is needed in subsequent blocks in the list item.  If the list
marker takes up two spaces, and there are three spaces between
the list marker and the next [non-whitespace character], then blocks
must be indented five spaces in order to fall under the list
item.

Here are some examples showing how far content must be indented to be
put under the list item:

```````````````````````````````` example
- one

 two
.
Document scope=1:1..3:4 children=2
├── List scope=1:1..2:0 flavor=bullet start=null tight=true children=1
│   └── ListItem scope=1:1..2:0 checked=null children=1
│       └── Paragraph scope=1:3..1:5 children=1
│           └── Text scope=1:3..1:5 literal="one" children=0
└── Paragraph scope=3:2..3:4 children=1
    └── Text scope=3:2..3:4 literal="two" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
- one

  two
.
Document scope=1:1..3:5 children=1
└── List scope=1:1..3:5 flavor=bullet start=null tight=false children=1
    └── ListItem scope=1:1..3:5 checked=null children=2
        ├── Paragraph scope=1:3..1:5 children=1
        │   └── Text scope=1:3..1:5 literal="one" children=0
        └── Paragraph scope=3:3..3:5 children=1
            └── Text scope=3:3..3:5 literal="two" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
 -    one

     two
.
Document scope=1:1..3:8 children=2
├── List scope=1:2..2:0 flavor=bullet start=null tight=true children=1
│   └── ListItem scope=1:2..2:0 checked=null children=1
│       └── Paragraph scope=1:7..1:9 children=1
│           └── Text scope=1:7..1:9 literal="one" children=0
└── CodeBlock scope=3:5..3:8 mode=standalone info=null language=null literal=" two\n" fenced=false closed=true children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
 -    one

      two
.
Document scope=1:1..3:9 children=1
└── List scope=1:2..3:9 flavor=bullet start=null tight=false children=1
    └── ListItem scope=1:2..3:9 checked=null children=2
        ├── Paragraph scope=1:7..1:9 children=1
        │   └── Text scope=1:7..1:9 literal="one" children=0
        └── Paragraph scope=3:7..3:9 children=1
            └── Text scope=3:7..3:9 literal="two" children=0
````````````````````````````````


It is tempting to think of this in terms of columns:  the continuation
blocks must be indented at least to the column of the first
[non-whitespace character] after the list marker. However, that is not quite right.
The spaces after the list marker determine how much relative indentation
is needed.  Which column this indentation reaches will depend on
how the list item is embedded in other constructions, as shown by
this example:

```````````````````````````````` example
   > > 1.  one
>>
>>     two
.
Document scope=1:1..3:10 children=1
└── BlockQuote scope=1:4..3:10 children=1
    └── BlockQuote scope=1:6..3:10 children=1
        └── List scope=1:8..3:10 flavor=ordered start=1 tight=false children=1
            └── ListItem scope=1:8..3:10 checked=null children=2
                ├── Paragraph scope=1:12..1:14 children=1
                │   └── Text scope=1:12..1:14 literal="one" children=0
                └── Paragraph scope=3:8..3:10 children=1
                    └── Text scope=3:8..3:10 literal="two" children=0
````````````````````````````````


Here `two` occurs in the same column as the list marker `1.`,
but is actually contained in the list item, because there is
sufficient indentation after the last containing blockquote marker.

The converse is also possible.  In the following example, the word `two`
occurs far to the right of the initial text of the list item, `one`, but
it is not considered part of the list item, because it is not indented
far enough past the blockquote marker:

```````````````````````````````` example
>>- one
>>
  >  > two
.
Document scope=1:1..3:10 children=1
└── BlockQuote scope=1:1..3:10 children=1
    └── BlockQuote scope=1:2..3:10 children=2
        ├── List scope=1:3..2:2 flavor=bullet start=null tight=true children=1
        │   └── ListItem scope=1:3..2:2 checked=null children=1
        │       └── Paragraph scope=1:5..1:7 children=1
        │           └── Text scope=1:5..1:7 literal="one" children=0
        └── Paragraph scope=3:8..3:10 children=1
            └── Text scope=3:8..3:10 literal="two" children=0
````````````````````````````````


Note that at least one space is needed between the list marker and
any following content, so these are not list items:

```````````````````````````````` example
-one

2.two
.
Document scope=1:1..3:5 children=2
├── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:4 children=1
│   └── Text scope=1:1..1:4 literal="-one" children=0
└── Paragraph scope=3:1..3:5 children=1
    └── Text scope=3:1..3:5 literal="2.two" children=0
````````````````````````````````


A list item may contain blocks that are separated by more than
one blank line.

```````````````````````````````` example
- foo


  bar
.
Document scope=1:1..4:5 children=1
└── List scope=1:1..4:5 flavor=bullet start=null tight=false children=1
    └── ListItem scope=1:1..4:5 checked=null children=2
        ├── Paragraph scope=1:3..1:5 children=1
        │   └── Text scope=1:3..1:5 literal="foo" children=0
        └── Paragraph scope=4:3..4:5 children=1
            └── Text scope=4:3..4:5 literal="bar" children=0
````````````````````````````````


A list item may contain any kind of block:

```````````````````````````````` example
1.  foo

    ```
    bar
    ```

    baz

    > bam
.
Document scope=1:1..9:9 children=1
└── List scope=1:1..9:9 flavor=ordered start=1 tight=false children=1
    └── ListItem scope=1:1..9:9 checked=null children=4
        ├── Paragraph scope=1:5..1:7 children=1
        │   └── Text scope=1:5..1:7 literal="foo" children=0
        ├── CodeBlock scope=3:5..5:7 mode=standalone info=null language=null literal="bar\n" fenced=true closed=true children=0
        ├── Paragraph scope=7:5..7:7 children=1
        │   └── Text scope=7:5..7:7 literal="baz" children=0
        └── BlockQuote scope=9:5..9:9 children=1
            └── Paragraph scope=9:7..9:9 children=1
                └── Text scope=9:7..9:9 literal="bam" children=0
````````````````````````````````


A list item that contains an indented code block will preserve
empty lines within the code block verbatim.

```````````````````````````````` example
- Foo

      bar


      baz
.
Document scope=1:1..6:9 children=1
└── List scope=1:1..6:9 flavor=bullet start=null tight=false children=1
    └── ListItem scope=1:1..6:9 checked=null children=2
        ├── Paragraph scope=1:3..1:5 children=1
        │   └── Text scope=1:3..1:5 literal="Foo" children=0
        └── CodeBlock scope=3:7..6:9 mode=standalone info=null language=null literal="bar\n\n\nbaz\n" fenced=false closed=true children=0
````````````````````````````````

Note that ordered list start numbers must be nine digits or less:

```````````````````````````````` example
123456789. ok
.
Document scope=1:1..1:13 children=1
└── List scope=1:1..1:13 flavor=ordered start=123456789 tight=true children=1
    └── ListItem scope=1:1..1:13 checked=null children=1
        └── Paragraph scope=1:12..1:13 children=1
            └── Text scope=1:12..1:13 literal="ok" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
1234567890. not ok
.
Document scope=1:1..1:18 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:18 children=1
    └── Text scope=1:1..1:18 literal="1234567890. not ok" children=0
````````````````````````````````


A start number may begin with 0s:

```````````````````````````````` example
0. ok
.
Document scope=1:1..1:5 children=1
└── List scope=1:1..1:5 flavor=ordered start=0 tight=true children=1
    └── ListItem scope=1:1..1:5 checked=null children=1
        └── Paragraph scope=1:4..1:5 children=1
            └── Text scope=1:4..1:5 literal="ok" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
003. ok
.
Document scope=1:1..1:7 children=1
└── List scope=1:1..1:7 flavor=ordered start=3 tight=true children=1
    └── ListItem scope=1:1..1:7 checked=null children=1
        └── Paragraph scope=1:6..1:7 children=1
            └── Text scope=1:6..1:7 literal="ok" children=0
````````````````````````````````


A start number may not be negative:

```````````````````````````````` example
-1. not ok
.
Document scope=1:1..1:10 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:10 children=1
    └── Text scope=1:1..1:10 literal="-1. not ok" children=0
````````````````````````````````



2.  **Item starting with indented code.**  If a sequence of lines *Ls*
    constitute a sequence of blocks *Bs* starting with an indented code
    block, and *M* is a list marker of width *W* followed by
    one space, then the result of prepending *M* and the following
    space to the first line of *Ls*, and indenting subsequent lines of
    *Ls* by *W + 1* spaces, is a list item with *Bs* as its contents.
    If a line is empty, then it need not be indented.  The type of the
    list item (bullet or ordered) is determined by the type of its list
    marker.  If the list item is ordered, then it is also assigned a
    start number, based on the ordered list marker.

An indented code block will have to be indented four spaces beyond
the edge of the region where text will be included in the list item.
In the following case that is 6 spaces:

```````````````````````````````` example
- foo

      bar
.
Document scope=1:1..3:9 children=1
└── List scope=1:1..3:9 flavor=bullet start=null tight=false children=1
    └── ListItem scope=1:1..3:9 checked=null children=2
        ├── Paragraph scope=1:3..1:5 children=1
        │   └── Text scope=1:3..1:5 literal="foo" children=0
        └── CodeBlock scope=3:7..3:9 mode=standalone info=null language=null literal="bar\n" fenced=false closed=true children=0
````````````````````````````````


And in this case it is 11 spaces:

```````````````````````````````` example
  10.  foo

           bar
.
Document scope=1:1..3:14 children=1
└── List scope=1:3..3:14 flavor=ordered start=10 tight=false children=1
    └── ListItem scope=1:3..3:14 checked=null children=2
        ├── Paragraph scope=1:8..1:10 children=1
        │   └── Text scope=1:8..1:10 literal="foo" children=0
        └── CodeBlock scope=3:12..3:14 mode=standalone info=null language=null literal="bar\n" fenced=false closed=true children=0
````````````````````````````````


If the *first* block in the list item is an indented code block,
then by rule #2, the contents must be indented *one* space after the
list marker:

```````````````````````````````` example
    indented code

paragraph

    more code
.
Document scope=1:1..5:13 children=3
├── CodeBlock scope=1:5..2:0 mode=standalone info=null language=null literal="indented code\n" fenced=false closed=true children=0
├── Paragraph scope=3:1..3:9 children=1
│   └── Text scope=3:1..3:9 literal="paragraph" children=0
└── CodeBlock scope=5:5..5:13 mode=standalone info=null language=null literal="more code\n" fenced=false closed=true children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
1.     indented code

   paragraph

       more code
.
Document scope=1:1..5:16 children=1
└── List scope=1:1..5:16 flavor=ordered start=1 tight=false children=1
    └── ListItem scope=1:1..5:16 checked=null children=3
        ├── CodeBlock scope=1:8..2:0 mode=standalone info=null language=null literal="indented code\n" fenced=false closed=true children=0
        ├── Paragraph scope=3:4..3:12 children=1
        │   └── Text scope=3:4..3:12 literal="paragraph" children=0
        └── CodeBlock scope=5:8..5:16 mode=standalone info=null language=null literal="more code\n" fenced=false closed=true children=0
````````````````````````````````


Note that an additional space indent is interpreted as space
inside the code block:

```````````````````````````````` example
1.      indented code

   paragraph

       more code
.
Document scope=1:1..5:16 children=1
└── List scope=1:1..5:16 flavor=ordered start=1 tight=false children=1
    └── ListItem scope=1:1..5:16 checked=null children=3
        ├── CodeBlock scope=1:8..2:0 mode=standalone info=null language=null literal=" indented code\n" fenced=false closed=true children=0
        ├── Paragraph scope=3:4..3:12 children=1
        │   └── Text scope=3:4..3:12 literal="paragraph" children=0
        └── CodeBlock scope=5:8..5:16 mode=standalone info=null language=null literal="more code\n" fenced=false closed=true children=0
````````````````````````````````


Note that rules #1 and #2 only apply to two cases:  (a) cases
in which the lines to be included in a list item begin with a
[non-whitespace character], and (b) cases in which
they begin with an indented code
block.  In a case like the following, where the first block begins with
a three-space indent, the rules do not allow us to form a list item by
indenting the whole thing and prepending a list marker:

```````````````````````````````` example
   foo

bar
.
Document scope=1:1..3:3 children=2
├── Paragraph scope=1:4..1:6 children=1
│   └── Text scope=1:4..1:6 literal="foo" children=0
└── Paragraph scope=3:1..3:3 children=1
    └── Text scope=3:1..3:3 literal="bar" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
-    foo

  bar
.
Document scope=1:1..3:5 children=2
├── List scope=1:1..2:0 flavor=bullet start=null tight=true children=1
│   └── ListItem scope=1:1..2:0 checked=null children=1
│       └── Paragraph scope=1:6..1:8 children=1
│           └── Text scope=1:6..1:8 literal="foo" children=0
└── Paragraph scope=3:3..3:5 children=1
    └── Text scope=3:3..3:5 literal="bar" children=0
````````````````````````````````


This is not a significant restriction, because when a block begins
with 1-3 spaces indent, the indentation can always be removed without
a change in interpretation, allowing rule #1 to be applied.  So, in
the above case:

```````````````````````````````` example
-  foo

   bar
.
Document scope=1:1..3:6 children=1
└── List scope=1:1..3:6 flavor=bullet start=null tight=false children=1
    └── ListItem scope=1:1..3:6 checked=null children=2
        ├── Paragraph scope=1:4..1:6 children=1
        │   └── Text scope=1:4..1:6 literal="foo" children=0
        └── Paragraph scope=3:4..3:6 children=1
            └── Text scope=3:4..3:6 literal="bar" children=0
````````````````````````````````


3.  **Item starting with a blank line.**  If a sequence of lines *Ls*
    starting with a single [blank line] constitute a (possibly empty)
    sequence of blocks *Bs*, not separated from each other by more than
    one blank line, and *M* is a list marker of width *W*,
    then the result of prepending *M* to the first line of *Ls*, and
    indenting subsequent lines of *Ls* by *W + 1* spaces, is a list
    item with *Bs* as its contents.
    If a line is empty, then it need not be indented.  The type of the
    list item (bullet or ordered) is determined by the type of its list
    marker.  If the list item is ordered, then it is also assigned a
    start number, based on the ordered list marker.

Here are some list items that start with a blank line but are not empty:

```````````````````````````````` example
-
  foo
-
  ```
  bar
  ```
-
      baz
.
Document scope=1:1..8:9 children=1
└── List scope=1:1..8:9 flavor=bullet start=null tight=true children=3
    ├── ListItem scope=1:1..2:5 checked=null children=1
    │   └── Paragraph scope=2:3..2:5 children=1
    │       └── Text scope=2:3..2:5 literal="foo" children=0
    ├── ListItem scope=3:1..6:5 checked=null children=1
    │   └── CodeBlock scope=4:3..6:5 mode=standalone info=null language=null literal="bar\n" fenced=true closed=true children=0
    └── ListItem scope=7:1..8:9 checked=null children=1
        └── CodeBlock scope=8:7..8:9 mode=standalone info=null language=null literal="baz\n" fenced=false closed=true children=0
````````````````````````````````

When the list item starts with a blank line, the number of spaces
following the list marker doesn't change the required indentation:

```````````````````````````````` example
-   
  foo
.
Document scope=1:1..2:5 children=1
└── List scope=1:1..2:5 flavor=bullet start=null tight=true children=1
    └── ListItem scope=1:1..2:5 checked=null children=1
        └── Paragraph scope=2:3..2:5 children=1
            └── Text scope=2:3..2:5 literal="foo" children=0
````````````````````````````````


A list item can begin with at most one blank line.
In the following example, `foo` is not part of the list
item:

```````````````````````````````` example
-

  foo
.
Document scope=1:1..3:5 children=2
├── List scope=1:1..2:0 flavor=bullet start=null tight=true children=1
│   └── ListItem scope=1:1..1:1 checked=null children=0
└── Paragraph scope=3:3..3:5 children=1
    └── Text scope=3:3..3:5 literal="foo" children=0
````````````````````````````````


Here is an empty bullet list item:

```````````````````````````````` example
- foo
-
- bar
.
Document scope=1:1..3:5 children=1
└── List scope=1:1..3:5 flavor=bullet start=null tight=true children=3
    ├── ListItem scope=1:1..1:5 checked=null children=1
    │   └── Paragraph scope=1:3..1:5 children=1
    │       └── Text scope=1:3..1:5 literal="foo" children=0
    ├── ListItem scope=2:1..2:1 checked=null children=0
    └── ListItem scope=3:1..3:5 checked=null children=1
        └── Paragraph scope=3:3..3:5 children=1
            └── Text scope=3:3..3:5 literal="bar" children=0
````````````````````````````````


It does not matter whether there are spaces following the [list marker]:

```````````````````````````````` example
- foo
-   
- bar
.
Document scope=1:1..3:5 children=1
└── List scope=1:1..3:5 flavor=bullet start=null tight=true children=3
    ├── ListItem scope=1:1..1:5 checked=null children=1
    │   └── Paragraph scope=1:3..1:5 children=1
    │       └── Text scope=1:3..1:5 literal="foo" children=0
    ├── ListItem scope=2:1..2:4 checked=null children=0
    └── ListItem scope=3:1..3:5 checked=null children=1
        └── Paragraph scope=3:3..3:5 children=1
            └── Text scope=3:3..3:5 literal="bar" children=0
````````````````````````````````


Here is an empty ordered list item:

```````````````````````````````` example
1. foo
2.
3. bar
.
Document scope=1:1..3:6 children=1
└── List scope=1:1..3:6 flavor=ordered start=1 tight=true children=3
    ├── ListItem scope=1:1..1:6 checked=null children=1
    │   └── Paragraph scope=1:4..1:6 children=1
    │       └── Text scope=1:4..1:6 literal="foo" children=0
    ├── ListItem scope=2:1..2:2 checked=null children=0
    └── ListItem scope=3:1..3:6 checked=null children=1
        └── Paragraph scope=3:4..3:6 children=1
            └── Text scope=3:4..3:6 literal="bar" children=0
````````````````````````````````


A list may start or end with an empty list item:

```````````````````````````````` example
*
.
Document scope=1:1..1:1 children=1
└── List scope=1:1..1:1 flavor=bullet start=null tight=true children=1
    └── ListItem scope=1:1..1:1 checked=null children=0
````````````````````````````````

However, an empty list item cannot interrupt a paragraph:

```````````````````````````````` example
foo
*

foo
1.
.
Document scope=1:1..5:2 children=2
├── Paragraph scope=1:1..2:1 children=3
│   ├── Text scope=1:1..1:3 literal="foo" children=0
│   ├── SoftBreak scope=0:0..0:0 children=0
│   └── Text scope=2:1..2:1 literal="*" children=0
└── Paragraph scope=4:1..5:2 children=3
    ├── Text scope=4:1..4:3 literal="foo" children=0
    ├── SoftBreak scope=0:0..0:0 children=0
    └── Text scope=5:1..5:2 literal="1." children=0
````````````````````````````````


4.  **Indentation.**  If a sequence of lines *Ls* constitutes a list item
    according to rule #1, #2, or #3, then the result of indenting each line
    of *Ls* by 1-3 spaces (the same for each line) also constitutes a
    list item with the same contents and attributes.  If a line is
    empty, then it need not be indented.

Indented one space:

```````````````````````````````` example
 1.  A paragraph
     with two lines.

         indented code

     > A block quote.
.
Document scope=1:1..6:21 children=1
└── List scope=1:2..6:21 flavor=ordered start=1 tight=false children=1
    └── ListItem scope=1:2..6:21 checked=null children=3
        ├── Paragraph scope=1:6..2:20 children=3
        │   ├── Text scope=1:6..1:16 literal="A paragraph" children=0
        │   ├── SoftBreak scope=0:0..0:0 children=0
        │   └── Text scope=2:6..2:20 literal="with two lines." children=0
        ├── CodeBlock scope=4:10..5:0 mode=standalone info=null language=null literal="indented code\n" fenced=false closed=true children=0
        └── BlockQuote scope=6:6..6:21 children=1
            └── Paragraph scope=6:8..6:21 children=1
                └── Text scope=6:8..6:21 literal="A block quote." children=0
````````````````````````````````


Indented two spaces:

```````````````````````````````` example
  1.  A paragraph
      with two lines.

          indented code

      > A block quote.
.
Document scope=1:1..6:22 children=1
└── List scope=1:3..6:22 flavor=ordered start=1 tight=false children=1
    └── ListItem scope=1:3..6:22 checked=null children=3
        ├── Paragraph scope=1:7..2:21 children=3
        │   ├── Text scope=1:7..1:17 literal="A paragraph" children=0
        │   ├── SoftBreak scope=0:0..0:0 children=0
        │   └── Text scope=2:7..2:21 literal="with two lines." children=0
        ├── CodeBlock scope=4:11..5:0 mode=standalone info=null language=null literal="indented code\n" fenced=false closed=true children=0
        └── BlockQuote scope=6:7..6:22 children=1
            └── Paragraph scope=6:9..6:22 children=1
                └── Text scope=6:9..6:22 literal="A block quote." children=0
````````````````````````````````


Indented three spaces:

```````````````````````````````` example
   1.  A paragraph
       with two lines.

           indented code

       > A block quote.
.
Document scope=1:1..6:23 children=1
└── List scope=1:4..6:23 flavor=ordered start=1 tight=false children=1
    └── ListItem scope=1:4..6:23 checked=null children=3
        ├── Paragraph scope=1:8..2:22 children=3
        │   ├── Text scope=1:8..1:18 literal="A paragraph" children=0
        │   ├── SoftBreak scope=0:0..0:0 children=0
        │   └── Text scope=2:8..2:22 literal="with two lines." children=0
        ├── CodeBlock scope=4:12..5:0 mode=standalone info=null language=null literal="indented code\n" fenced=false closed=true children=0
        └── BlockQuote scope=6:8..6:23 children=1
            └── Paragraph scope=6:10..6:23 children=1
                └── Text scope=6:10..6:23 literal="A block quote." children=0
````````````````````````````````


Four spaces indent gives a code block:

```````````````````````````````` example
    1.  A paragraph
        with two lines.

            indented code

        > A block quote.
.
Document scope=1:1..6:24 children=1
└── CodeBlock scope=1:5..6:24 mode=standalone info=null language=null literal="1.  A paragraph\n    with two lines.\n\n        indented code\n\n    > A block quote.\n" fenced=false closed=true children=0
````````````````````````````````



5.  **Laziness.**  If a string of lines *Ls* constitute a [list
    item](#list-items) with contents *Bs*, then the result of deleting
    some or all of the indentation from one or more lines in which the
    next [non-whitespace character] after the indentation is
    [paragraph continuation text] is a
    list item with the same contents and attributes.  The unindented
    lines are called
    [lazy continuation line](@)s.

Here is an example with [lazy continuation lines]:

```````````````````````````````` example
  1.  A paragraph
with two lines.

          indented code

      > A block quote.
.
Document scope=1:1..6:22 children=1
└── List scope=1:3..6:22 flavor=ordered start=1 tight=false children=1
    └── ListItem scope=1:3..6:22 checked=null children=3
        ├── Paragraph scope=1:7..2:15 children=3
        │   ├── Text scope=1:7..1:17 literal="A paragraph" children=0
        │   ├── SoftBreak scope=0:0..0:0 children=0
        │   └── Text scope=2:7..2:21 literal="with two lines." children=0
        ├── CodeBlock scope=4:11..5:0 mode=standalone info=null language=null literal="indented code\n" fenced=false closed=true children=0
        └── BlockQuote scope=6:7..6:22 children=1
            └── Paragraph scope=6:9..6:22 children=1
                └── Text scope=6:9..6:22 literal="A block quote." children=0
````````````````````````````````


Indentation can be partially deleted:

```````````````````````````````` example
  1.  A paragraph
    with two lines.
.
Document scope=1:1..2:19 children=1
└── List scope=1:3..2:19 flavor=ordered start=1 tight=true children=1
    └── ListItem scope=1:3..2:19 checked=null children=1
        └── Paragraph scope=1:7..2:19 children=3
            ├── Text scope=1:7..1:17 literal="A paragraph" children=0
            ├── SoftBreak scope=0:0..0:0 children=0
            └── Text scope=2:11..2:25 literal="with two lines." children=0
````````````````````````````````


These examples show how laziness can work in nested structures:

```````````````````````````````` example
> 1. > Blockquote
continued here.
.
Document scope=1:1..2:15 children=1
└── BlockQuote scope=1:1..2:15 children=1
    └── List scope=1:3..2:15 flavor=ordered start=1 tight=true children=1
        └── ListItem scope=1:3..2:15 checked=null children=1
            └── BlockQuote scope=1:6..2:15 children=1
                └── Paragraph scope=1:8..2:15 children=3
                    ├── Text scope=1:8..1:17 literal="Blockquote" children=0
                    ├── SoftBreak scope=0:0..0:0 children=0
                    └── Text scope=2:8..2:22 literal="continued here." children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
> 1. > Blockquote
> continued here.
.
Document scope=1:1..2:17 children=1
└── BlockQuote scope=1:1..2:17 children=1
    └── List scope=1:3..2:17 flavor=ordered start=1 tight=true children=1
        └── ListItem scope=1:3..2:17 checked=null children=1
            └── BlockQuote scope=1:6..2:17 children=1
                └── Paragraph scope=1:8..2:17 children=3
                    ├── Text scope=1:8..1:17 literal="Blockquote" children=0
                    ├── SoftBreak scope=0:0..0:0 children=0
                    └── Text scope=2:8..2:22 literal="continued here." children=0
````````````````````````````````



6.  **That's all.** Nothing that is not counted as a list item by rules
    #1--5 counts as a [list item](#list-items).

The rules for sublists follow from the general rules
[above][List items].  A sublist must be indented the same number
of spaces a paragraph would need to be in order to be included
in the list item.

So, in this case we need two spaces indent:

```````````````````````````````` example
- foo
  - bar
    - baz
      - boo
.
Document scope=1:1..4:11 children=1
└── List scope=1:1..4:11 flavor=bullet start=null tight=true children=1
    └── ListItem scope=1:1..4:11 checked=null children=2
        ├── Paragraph scope=1:3..1:5 children=1
        │   └── Text scope=1:3..1:5 literal="foo" children=0
        └── List scope=2:3..4:11 flavor=bullet start=null tight=true children=1
            └── ListItem scope=2:3..4:11 checked=null children=2
                ├── Paragraph scope=2:5..2:7 children=1
                │   └── Text scope=2:5..2:7 literal="bar" children=0
                └── List scope=3:5..4:11 flavor=bullet start=null tight=true children=1
                    └── ListItem scope=3:5..4:11 checked=null children=2
                        ├── Paragraph scope=3:7..3:9 children=1
                        │   └── Text scope=3:7..3:9 literal="baz" children=0
                        └── List scope=4:7..4:11 flavor=bullet start=null tight=true children=1
                            └── ListItem scope=4:7..4:11 checked=null children=1
                                └── Paragraph scope=4:9..4:11 children=1
                                    └── Text scope=4:9..4:11 literal="boo" children=0
````````````````````````````````


One is not enough:

```````````````````````````````` example
- foo
 - bar
  - baz
   - boo
.
Document scope=1:1..4:8 children=1
└── List scope=1:1..4:8 flavor=bullet start=null tight=true children=4
    ├── ListItem scope=1:1..1:5 checked=null children=1
    │   └── Paragraph scope=1:3..1:5 children=1
    │       └── Text scope=1:3..1:5 literal="foo" children=0
    ├── ListItem scope=2:2..2:6 checked=null children=1
    │   └── Paragraph scope=2:4..2:6 children=1
    │       └── Text scope=2:4..2:6 literal="bar" children=0
    ├── ListItem scope=3:3..3:7 checked=null children=1
    │   └── Paragraph scope=3:5..3:7 children=1
    │       └── Text scope=3:5..3:7 literal="baz" children=0
    └── ListItem scope=4:4..4:8 checked=null children=1
        └── Paragraph scope=4:6..4:8 children=1
            └── Text scope=4:6..4:8 literal="boo" children=0
````````````````````````````````


Here we need four, because the list marker is wider:

```````````````````````````````` example
10) foo
    - bar
.
Document scope=1:1..2:9 children=1
└── List scope=1:1..2:9 flavor=ordered start=10 tight=true children=1
    └── ListItem scope=1:1..2:9 checked=null children=2
        ├── Paragraph scope=1:5..1:7 children=1
        │   └── Text scope=1:5..1:7 literal="foo" children=0
        └── List scope=2:5..2:9 flavor=bullet start=null tight=true children=1
            └── ListItem scope=2:5..2:9 checked=null children=1
