```````````````````````````````` example
        ├── Emphasis scope=2:1..2:22 children=1
        │   └── Text scope=2:2..2:21 literal="Asclepias physocarpa" children=0
        └── Text scope=2:23..2:23 literal=")" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
**foo "*bar*" foo**
.
Document scope=1:1..1:19 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:19 children=1
    └── Strong scope=1:1..1:19 children=3
        ├── Text scope=1:3..1:7 literal="foo \"" children=0
        ├── Emphasis scope=1:8..1:12 children=1
        │   └── Text scope=1:9..1:11 literal="bar" children=0
        └── Text scope=1:13..1:17 literal="\" foo" children=0
````````````````````````````````


Intraword emphasis:

```````````````````````````````` example
**foo**bar
.
Document scope=1:1..1:10 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:10 children=2
    ├── Strong scope=1:1..1:7 children=1
    │   └── Text scope=1:3..1:5 literal="foo" children=0
    └── Text scope=1:8..1:10 literal="bar" children=0
````````````````````````````````


Rule 8:

This is not strong emphasis, because the closing delimiter is
preceded by whitespace:

```````````````````````````````` example
__foo bar __
.
Document scope=1:1..1:12 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:12 children=1
    └── Text scope=1:1..1:12 literal="__foo bar __" children=0
````````````````````````````````


This is not strong emphasis, because the second `__` is
preceded by punctuation and followed by an alphanumeric:

```````````````````````````````` example
__(__foo)
.
Document scope=1:1..1:9 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:9 children=1
    └── Text scope=1:1..1:9 literal="__(__foo)" children=0
````````````````````````````````


The point of this restriction is more easily appreciated
with this example:

```````````````````````````````` example
_(__foo__)_
.
Document scope=1:1..1:11 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:11 children=1
    └── Emphasis scope=1:1..1:11 children=3
        ├── Text scope=1:2..1:2 literal="(" children=0
        ├── Strong scope=1:3..1:9 children=1
        │   └── Text scope=1:5..1:7 literal="foo" children=0
        └── Text scope=1:10..1:10 literal=")" children=0
````````````````````````````````


Intraword strong emphasis is forbidden with `__`:

```````````````````````````````` example
__foo__bar
.
Document scope=1:1..1:10 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:10 children=1
    └── Text scope=1:1..1:10 literal="__foo__bar" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
__пристаням__стремятся
.
Document scope=1:1..1:40 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:40 children=1
    └── Text scope=1:1..1:40 literal="__пристаням__стремятся" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
__foo__bar__baz__
.
Document scope=1:1..1:17 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:17 children=1
    └── Strong scope=1:1..1:17 children=1
        └── Text scope=1:3..1:15 literal="foo__bar__baz" children=0
````````````````````````````````


This is strong emphasis, even though the closing delimiter is
both left- and right-flanking, because it is followed by
punctuation:

```````````````````````````````` example
__(bar)__.
.
Document scope=1:1..1:10 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:10 children=2
    ├── Strong scope=1:1..1:9 children=1
    │   └── Text scope=1:3..1:7 literal="(bar)" children=0
    └── Text scope=1:10..1:10 literal="." children=0
````````````````````````````````


Rule 9:

Any nonempty sequence of inline elements can be the contents of an
emphasized span.

```````````````````````````````` example
*foo [bar](/url)*
.
Document scope=1:1..1:17 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:17 children=1
    └── Emphasis scope=1:1..1:17 children=2
        ├── Text scope=1:2..1:5 literal="foo " children=0
        └── Link scope=1:6..1:16 destination="/url" title=null children=1
            └── Text scope=1:7..1:9 literal="bar" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
*foo
bar*
.
Document scope=1:1..2:4 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..2:4 children=1
    └── Emphasis scope=1:1..2:4 children=3
        ├── Text scope=1:2..1:4 literal="foo" children=0
        ├── SoftBreak scope=0:0..0:0 children=0
        └── Text scope=2:1..2:3 literal="bar" children=0
````````````````````````````````


In particular, emphasis and strong emphasis can be nested
inside emphasis:

```````````````````````````````` example
_foo __bar__ baz_
.
Document scope=1:1..1:17 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:17 children=1
    └── Emphasis scope=1:1..1:17 children=3
        ├── Text scope=1:2..1:5 literal="foo " children=0
        ├── Strong scope=1:6..1:12 children=1
        │   └── Text scope=1:8..1:10 literal="bar" children=0
        └── Text scope=1:13..1:16 literal=" baz" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
_foo _bar_ baz_
.
Document scope=1:1..1:15 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:15 children=1
    └── Emphasis scope=1:1..1:15 children=3
        ├── Text scope=1:2..1:5 literal="foo " children=0
        ├── Emphasis scope=1:6..1:10 children=1
        │   └── Text scope=1:7..1:9 literal="bar" children=0
        └── Text scope=1:11..1:14 literal=" baz" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
__foo_ bar_
.
Document scope=1:1..1:11 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:11 children=1
    └── Emphasis scope=1:1..1:11 children=2
        ├── Emphasis scope=1:1..1:6 children=1
        │   └── Text scope=1:3..1:5 literal="foo" children=0
        └── Text scope=1:7..1:10 literal=" bar" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
*foo *bar**
.
Document scope=1:1..1:11 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:11 children=1
    └── Emphasis scope=1:1..1:11 children=2
        ├── Text scope=1:2..1:5 literal="foo " children=0
        └── Emphasis scope=1:6..1:11 children=1
            └── Text scope=1:7..1:9 literal="bar" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
*foo **bar** baz*
.
Document scope=1:1..1:17 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:17 children=1
    └── Emphasis scope=1:1..1:17 children=3
        ├── Text scope=1:2..1:5 literal="foo " children=0
        ├── Strong scope=1:6..1:12 children=1
        │   └── Text scope=1:8..1:10 literal="bar" children=0
        └── Text scope=1:13..1:16 literal=" baz" children=0
````````````````````````````````

```````````````````````````````` example
*foo**bar**baz*
.
Document scope=1:1..1:15 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:15 children=1
    └── Emphasis scope=1:1..1:15 children=3
        ├── Text scope=1:2..1:4 literal="foo" children=0
        ├── Strong scope=1:5..1:11 children=1
        │   └── Text scope=1:7..1:9 literal="bar" children=0
        └── Text scope=1:12..1:14 literal="baz" children=0
````````````````````````````````

Note that in the preceding case, the interpretation

``` markdown
<p><em>foo</em><em>bar<em></em>baz</em></p>
```


is precluded by the condition that a delimiter that
can both open and close (like the `*` after `foo`)
cannot form emphasis if the sum of the lengths of
the delimiter runs containing the opening and
closing delimiters is a multiple of 3 unless
both lengths are multiples of 3.


For the same reason, we don't get two consecutive
emphasis sections in this example:

```````````````````````````````` example
*foo**bar*
.
Document scope=1:1..1:10 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:10 children=1
    └── Emphasis scope=1:1..1:10 children=1
        └── Text scope=1:2..1:9 literal="foo**bar" children=0
````````````````````````````````


The same condition ensures that the following
cases are all strong emphasis nested inside
emphasis, even when the interior spaces are
omitted:


```````````````````````````````` example
***foo** bar*
.
Document scope=1:1..1:13 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:13 children=1
    └── Emphasis scope=1:1..1:13 children=2
        ├── Strong scope=1:1..1:8 children=1
        │   └── Text scope=1:4..1:6 literal="foo" children=0
        └── Text scope=1:9..1:12 literal=" bar" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
*foo **bar***
.
Document scope=1:1..1:13 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:13 children=1
    └── Emphasis scope=1:1..1:13 children=2
        ├── Text scope=1:2..1:5 literal="foo " children=0
        └── Strong scope=1:6..1:13 children=1
            └── Text scope=1:8..1:10 literal="bar" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
*foo**bar***
.
Document scope=1:1..1:12 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:12 children=1
    └── Emphasis scope=1:1..1:12 children=2
        ├── Text scope=1:2..1:4 literal="foo" children=0
        └── Strong scope=1:5..1:12 children=1
            └── Text scope=1:7..1:9 literal="bar" children=0
````````````````````````````````


When the lengths of the interior closing and opening
delimiter runs are *both* multiples of 3, though,
they can match to create emphasis:

```````````````````````````````` example
foo***bar***baz
.
Document scope=1:1..1:15 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:15 children=3
    ├── Text scope=1:1..1:3 literal="foo" children=0
    ├── Emphasis scope=1:4..1:12 children=1
    │   └── Strong scope=1:4..1:12 children=1
    │       └── Text scope=1:7..1:9 literal="bar" children=0
    └── Text scope=1:13..1:15 literal="baz" children=0
````````````````````````````````

```````````````````````````````` example
foo******bar*********baz
.
Document scope=1:1..1:24 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:24 children=3
    ├── Text scope=1:1..1:3 literal="foo" children=0
    ├── Strong scope=1:4..1:21 children=1
    │   └── Strong scope=1:4..1:21 children=1
    │       └── Strong scope=1:4..1:21 children=1
    │           └── Text scope=1:10..1:12 literal="bar" children=0
    └── Text scope=1:13..1:24 literal="***baz" children=0
````````````````````````````````


Indefinite levels of nesting are possible:

```````````````````````````````` example
*foo **bar *baz* bim** bop*
.
Document scope=1:1..1:27 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:27 children=1
    └── Emphasis scope=1:1..1:27 children=3
        ├── Text scope=1:2..1:5 literal="foo " children=0
        ├── Strong scope=1:6..1:22 children=3
        │   ├── Text scope=1:8..1:11 literal="bar " children=0
        │   ├── Emphasis scope=1:12..1:16 children=1
        │   │   └── Text scope=1:13..1:15 literal="baz" children=0
        │   └── Text scope=1:17..1:20 literal=" bim" children=0
        └── Text scope=1:23..1:26 literal=" bop" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
*foo [*bar*](/url)*
.
Document scope=1:1..1:19 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:19 children=1
    └── Emphasis scope=1:1..1:19 children=2
        ├── Text scope=1:2..1:5 literal="foo " children=0
        └── Link scope=1:6..1:18 destination="/url" title=null children=1
            └── Emphasis scope=1:7..1:11 children=1
                └── Text scope=1:8..1:10 literal="bar" children=0
````````````````````````````````


There can be no empty emphasis or strong emphasis:

```````````````````````````````` example
** is not an empty emphasis
.
Document scope=1:1..1:27 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:27 children=1
    └── Text scope=1:1..1:27 literal="** is not an empty emphasis" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
**** is not an empty strong emphasis
.
Document scope=1:1..1:36 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:36 children=1
    └── Text scope=1:1..1:36 literal="**** is not an empty strong emphasis" children=0
````````````````````````````````



Rule 10:

Any nonempty sequence of inline elements can be the contents of an
strongly emphasized span.

```````````````````````````````` example
**foo [bar](/url)**
.
Document scope=1:1..1:19 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:19 children=1
    └── Strong scope=1:1..1:19 children=2
        ├── Text scope=1:3..1:6 literal="foo " children=0
        └── Link scope=1:7..1:17 destination="/url" title=null children=1
            └── Text scope=1:8..1:10 literal="bar" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
**foo
bar**
.
Document scope=1:1..2:5 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..2:5 children=1
    └── Strong scope=1:1..2:5 children=3
        ├── Text scope=1:3..1:5 literal="foo" children=0
        ├── SoftBreak scope=0:0..0:0 children=0
        └── Text scope=2:1..2:3 literal="bar" children=0
````````````````````````````````


In particular, emphasis and strong emphasis can be nested
inside strong emphasis:

```````````````````````````````` example
__foo _bar_ baz__
.
Document scope=1:1..1:17 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:17 children=1
    └── Strong scope=1:1..1:17 children=3
        ├── Text scope=1:3..1:6 literal="foo " children=0
        ├── Emphasis scope=1:7..1:11 children=1
        │   └── Text scope=1:8..1:10 literal="bar" children=0
        └── Text scope=1:12..1:15 literal=" baz" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
__foo __bar__ baz__
.
Document scope=1:1..1:19 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:19 children=1
    └── Strong scope=1:1..1:19 children=3
        ├── Text scope=1:3..1:6 literal="foo " children=0
        ├── Strong scope=1:7..1:13 children=1
        │   └── Text scope=1:9..1:11 literal="bar" children=0
        └── Text scope=1:14..1:17 literal=" baz" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
____foo__ bar__
.
Document scope=1:1..1:15 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:15 children=1
    └── Strong scope=1:1..1:15 children=2
        ├── Strong scope=1:1..1:9 children=1
        │   └── Text scope=1:5..1:7 literal="foo" children=0
        └── Text scope=1:10..1:13 literal=" bar" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
**foo **bar****
.
Document scope=1:1..1:15 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:15 children=1
    └── Strong scope=1:1..1:15 children=2
        ├── Text scope=1:3..1:6 literal="foo " children=0
        └── Strong scope=1:7..1:15 children=1
            └── Text scope=1:9..1:11 literal="bar" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
**foo *bar* baz**
.
Document scope=1:1..1:17 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:17 children=1
    └── Strong scope=1:1..1:17 children=3
        ├── Text scope=1:3..1:6 literal="foo " children=0
        ├── Emphasis scope=1:7..1:11 children=1
        │   └── Text scope=1:8..1:10 literal="bar" children=0
        └── Text scope=1:12..1:15 literal=" baz" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
**foo*bar*baz**
.
Document scope=1:1..1:15 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:15 children=1
    └── Strong scope=1:1..1:15 children=3
        ├── Text scope=1:3..1:5 literal="foo" children=0
        ├── Emphasis scope=1:6..1:10 children=1
        │   └── Text scope=1:7..1:9 literal="bar" children=0
        └── Text scope=1:11..1:13 literal="baz" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
***foo* bar**
.
Document scope=1:1..1:13 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:13 children=1
    └── Strong scope=1:1..1:13 children=2
        ├── Emphasis scope=1:1..1:7 children=1
        │   └── Text scope=1:4..1:6 literal="foo" children=0
        └── Text scope=1:8..1:11 literal=" bar" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
**foo *bar***
.
Document scope=1:1..1:13 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:13 children=1
    └── Strong scope=1:1..1:13 children=2
        ├── Text scope=1:3..1:6 literal="foo " children=0
        └── Emphasis scope=1:7..1:13 children=1
            └── Text scope=1:8..1:10 literal="bar" children=0
````````````````````````````````


Indefinite levels of nesting are possible:

```````````````````````````````` example
**foo *bar **baz**
bim* bop**
.
Document scope=1:1..2:10 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..2:10 children=1
    └── Strong scope=1:1..2:10 children=3
        ├── Text scope=1:3..1:6 literal="foo " children=0
        ├── Emphasis scope=1:7..2:4 children=4
        │   ├── Text scope=1:8..1:11 literal="bar " children=0
        │   ├── Strong scope=1:12..1:18 children=1
        │   │   └── Text scope=1:14..1:16 literal="baz" children=0
        │   ├── SoftBreak scope=0:0..0:0 children=0
        │   └── Text scope=2:1..2:3 literal="bim" children=0
        └── Text scope=2:5..2:8 literal=" bop" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
**foo [*bar*](/url)**
.
Document scope=1:1..1:21 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:21 children=1
    └── Strong scope=1:1..1:21 children=2
        ├── Text scope=1:3..1:6 literal="foo " children=0
        └── Link scope=1:7..1:19 destination="/url" title=null children=1
            └── Emphasis scope=1:8..1:12 children=1
                └── Text scope=1:9..1:11 literal="bar" children=0
````````````````````````````````


There can be no empty emphasis or strong emphasis:

```````````````````````````````` example
__ is not an empty emphasis
.
Document scope=1:1..1:27 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:27 children=1
    └── Text scope=1:1..1:27 literal="__ is not an empty emphasis" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
____ is not an empty strong emphasis
.
Document scope=1:1..1:36 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:36 children=1
    └── Text scope=1:1..1:36 literal="____ is not an empty strong emphasis" children=0
````````````````````````````````



Rule 11:

```````````````````````````````` example
foo ***
.
Document scope=1:1..1:7 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:7 children=1
    └── Text scope=1:1..1:7 literal="foo ***" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
foo *\**
.
Document scope=1:1..1:8 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:8 children=2
    ├── Text scope=1:1..1:4 literal="foo " children=0
    └── Emphasis scope=1:5..1:8 children=1
        └── Text scope=1:6..1:7 literal="*" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
foo *_*
.
Document scope=1:1..1:7 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:7 children=2
    ├── Text scope=1:1..1:4 literal="foo " children=0
    └── Emphasis scope=1:5..1:7 children=1
        └── Text scope=1:6..1:6 literal="_" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
foo *****
.
Document scope=1:1..1:9 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:9 children=1
    └── Text scope=1:1..1:9 literal="foo *****" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
foo **\***
.
Document scope=1:1..1:10 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:10 children=2
    ├── Text scope=1:1..1:4 literal="foo " children=0
    └── Strong scope=1:5..1:10 children=1
        └── Text scope=1:7..1:8 literal="*" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
foo **_**
.
Document scope=1:1..1:9 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:9 children=2
    ├── Text scope=1:1..1:4 literal="foo " children=0
    └── Strong scope=1:5..1:9 children=1
        └── Text scope=1:7..1:7 literal="_" children=0
````````````````````````````````


Note that when delimiters do not match evenly, Rule 11 determines
that the excess literal `*` characters will appear outside of the
emphasis, rather than inside it:

```````````````````````````````` example
**foo*
.
Document scope=1:1..1:6 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:6 children=2
    ├── Text scope=1:1..1:2 literal="*" children=0
    └── Emphasis scope=1:1..1:6 children=1
        └── Text scope=1:3..1:5 literal="foo" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
*foo**
.
Document scope=1:1..1:6 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:6 children=2
    ├── Emphasis scope=1:1..1:6 children=1
    │   └── Text scope=1:2..1:4 literal="foo" children=0
    └── Text scope=1:5..1:6 literal="*" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
***foo**
.
Document scope=1:1..1:8 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:8 children=2
    ├── Text scope=1:1..1:3 literal="*" children=0
    └── Strong scope=1:1..1:8 children=1
        └── Text scope=1:4..1:6 literal="foo" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
****foo*
.
Document scope=1:1..1:8 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:8 children=2
    ├── Text scope=1:1..1:4 literal="***" children=0
    └── Emphasis scope=1:1..1:8 children=1
        └── Text scope=1:5..1:7 literal="foo" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
**foo***
.
Document scope=1:1..1:8 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:8 children=2
    ├── Strong scope=1:1..1:8 children=1
    │   └── Text scope=1:3..1:5 literal="foo" children=0
    └── Text scope=1:6..1:8 literal="*" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
*foo****
.
Document scope=1:1..1:8 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:8 children=2
    ├── Emphasis scope=1:1..1:8 children=1
    │   └── Text scope=1:2..1:4 literal="foo" children=0
    └── Text scope=1:5..1:8 literal="***" children=0
````````````````````````````````



Rule 12:

```````````````````````````````` example
foo ___
.
Document scope=1:1..1:7 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:7 children=1
    └── Text scope=1:1..1:7 literal="foo ___" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
foo _\__
.
Document scope=1:1..1:8 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:8 children=2
    ├── Text scope=1:1..1:4 literal="foo " children=0
    └── Emphasis scope=1:5..1:8 children=1
        └── Text scope=1:6..1:7 literal="_" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
foo _*_
.
Document scope=1:1..1:7 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:7 children=2
    ├── Text scope=1:1..1:4 literal="foo " children=0
    └── Emphasis scope=1:5..1:7 children=1
        └── Text scope=1:6..1:6 literal="*" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
foo _____
.
Document scope=1:1..1:9 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:9 children=1
    └── Text scope=1:1..1:9 literal="foo _____" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
foo __\___
.
Document scope=1:1..1:10 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:10 children=2
    ├── Text scope=1:1..1:4 literal="foo " children=0
    └── Strong scope=1:5..1:10 children=1
        └── Text scope=1:7..1:8 literal="_" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
foo __*__
.
Document scope=1:1..1:9 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:9 children=2
    ├── Text scope=1:1..1:4 literal="foo " children=0
    └── Strong scope=1:5..1:9 children=1
        └── Text scope=1:7..1:7 literal="*" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
__foo_
.
Document scope=1:1..1:6 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:6 children=2
    ├── Text scope=1:1..1:2 literal="_" children=0
    └── Emphasis scope=1:1..1:6 children=1
        └── Text scope=1:3..1:5 literal="foo" children=0
````````````````````````````````


Note that when delimiters do not match evenly, Rule 12 determines
that the excess literal `_` characters will appear outside of the
emphasis, rather than inside it:

```````````````````````````````` example
_foo__
.
Document scope=1:1..1:6 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:6 children=2
    ├── Emphasis scope=1:1..1:6 children=1
    │   └── Text scope=1:2..1:4 literal="foo" children=0
    └── Text scope=1:5..1:6 literal="_" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
___foo__
.
Document scope=1:1..1:8 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:8 children=2
    ├── Text scope=1:1..1:3 literal="_" children=0
    └── Strong scope=1:1..1:8 children=1
        └── Text scope=1:4..1:6 literal="foo" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
____foo_
.
Document scope=1:1..1:8 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:8 children=2
    ├── Text scope=1:1..1:4 literal="___" children=0
    └── Emphasis scope=1:1..1:8 children=1
        └── Text scope=1:5..1:7 literal="foo" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
__foo___
.
Document scope=1:1..1:8 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:8 children=2
    ├── Strong scope=1:1..1:8 children=1
    │   └── Text scope=1:3..1:5 literal="foo" children=0
    └── Text scope=1:6..1:8 literal="_" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
_foo____
.
Document scope=1:1..1:8 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:8 children=2
    ├── Emphasis scope=1:1..1:8 children=1
    │   └── Text scope=1:2..1:4 literal="foo" children=0
    └── Text scope=1:5..1:8 literal="___" children=0
````````````````````````````````


Rule 13 implies that if you want emphasis nested directly inside
emphasis, you must use different delimiters:

```````````````````````````````` example
**foo**
.
Document scope=1:1..1:7 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:7 children=1
    └── Strong scope=1:1..1:7 children=1
        └── Text scope=1:3..1:5 literal="foo" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
*_foo_*
.
Document scope=1:1..1:7 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:7 children=1
    └── Emphasis scope=1:1..1:7 children=1
        └── Emphasis scope=1:2..1:6 children=1
            └── Text scope=1:3..1:5 literal="foo" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
__foo__
.
Document scope=1:1..1:7 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:7 children=1
    └── Strong scope=1:1..1:7 children=1
        └── Text scope=1:3..1:5 literal="foo" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
_*foo*_
.
Document scope=1:1..1:7 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:7 children=1
    └── Emphasis scope=1:1..1:7 children=1
        └── Emphasis scope=1:2..1:6 children=1
            └── Text scope=1:3..1:5 literal="foo" children=0
````````````````````````````````


However, strong emphasis within strong emphasis is possible without
switching delimiters:

```````````````````````````````` example
****foo****
.
Document scope=1:1..1:11 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:11 children=1
    └── Strong scope=1:1..1:11 children=1
        └── Strong scope=1:1..1:11 children=1
            └── Text scope=1:5..1:7 literal="foo" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
____foo____
.
Document scope=1:1..1:11 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:11 children=1
    └── Strong scope=1:1..1:11 children=1
        └── Strong scope=1:1..1:11 children=1
            └── Text scope=1:5..1:7 literal="foo" children=0
````````````````````````````````



Rule 13 can be applied to arbitrarily long sequences of
delimiters:

```````````````````````````````` example
******foo******
.
Document scope=1:1..1:15 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:15 children=1
    └── Strong scope=1:1..1:15 children=1
        └── Strong scope=1:1..1:15 children=1
            └── Strong scope=1:1..1:15 children=1
                └── Text scope=1:7..1:9 literal="foo" children=0
````````````````````````````````


Rule 14:

```````````````````````````````` example
***foo***
.
Document scope=1:1..1:9 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:9 children=1
    └── Emphasis scope=1:1..1:9 children=1
        └── Strong scope=1:1..1:9 children=1
            └── Text scope=1:4..1:6 literal="foo" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
_____foo_____
.
Document scope=1:1..1:13 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:13 children=1
    └── Emphasis scope=1:1..1:13 children=1
        └── Strong scope=1:1..1:13 children=1
            └── Strong scope=1:1..1:13 children=1
                └── Text scope=1:6..1:8 literal="foo" children=0
````````````````````````````````


Rule 15:

```````````````````````````````` example
*foo _bar* baz_
.
Document scope=1:1..1:15 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:15 children=2
    ├── Emphasis scope=1:1..1:10 children=1
    │   └── Text scope=1:2..1:9 literal="foo _bar" children=0
    └── Text scope=1:11..1:15 literal=" baz_" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
*foo __bar *baz bim__ bam*
.
Document scope=1:1..1:26 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:26 children=1
    └── Emphasis scope=1:1..1:26 children=3
        ├── Text scope=1:2..1:5 literal="foo " children=0
        ├── Strong scope=1:6..1:21 children=1
        │   └── Text scope=1:8..1:19 literal="bar *baz bim" children=0
        └── Text scope=1:22..1:25 literal=" bam" children=0
````````````````````````````````


Rule 16:

```````````````````````````````` example
**foo **bar baz**
.
Document scope=1:1..1:17 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:17 children=2
    ├── Text scope=1:1..1:6 literal="**foo " children=0
    └── Strong scope=1:7..1:17 children=1
        └── Text scope=1:9..1:15 literal="bar baz" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
*foo *bar baz*
.
Document scope=1:1..1:14 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:14 children=2
    ├── Text scope=1:1..1:5 literal="*foo " children=0
    └── Emphasis scope=1:6..1:14 children=1
        └── Text scope=1:7..1:13 literal="bar baz" children=0
````````````````````````````````


Rule 17:

```````````````````````````````` example
*[bar*](/url)
.
Document scope=1:1..1:13 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:13 children=2
    ├── Text scope=1:1..1:1 literal="*" children=0
    └── Link scope=1:2..1:13 destination="/url" title=null children=1
        └── Text scope=1:3..1:6 literal="bar*" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
_foo [bar_](/url)
.
Document scope=1:1..1:17 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:17 children=2
    ├── Text scope=1:1..1:5 literal="_foo " children=0
    └── Link scope=1:6..1:17 destination="/url" title=null children=1
        └── Text scope=1:7..1:10 literal="bar_" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
*<img src="foo" title="*"/>
.
Document scope=1:1..1:27 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:27 children=2
    ├── Text scope=1:1..1:1 literal="*" children=0
    └── HTML scope=1:2..1:27 literal="<img src=\"foo\" title=\"*\"/>" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
**<a href="**">
.
Document scope=1:1..1:15 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:15 children=2
    ├── Text scope=1:1..1:2 literal="**" children=0
    └── HTML scope=1:3..1:15 literal="<a href=\"**\">" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
__<a href="__">
.
Document scope=1:1..1:15 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:15 children=2
    ├── Text scope=1:1..1:2 literal="__" children=0
    └── HTML scope=1:3..1:15 literal="<a href=\"__\">" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
*a `*`*
.
Document scope=1:1..1:7 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:7 children=1
    └── Emphasis scope=1:1..1:7 children=2
        ├── Text scope=1:2..1:3 literal="a " children=0
        └── Code scope=1:5..1:5 mode=embedded literal="*" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
_a `_`_
.
Document scope=1:1..1:7 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:7 children=1
    └── Emphasis scope=1:1..1:7 children=2
        ├── Text scope=1:2..1:3 literal="a " children=0
        └── Code scope=1:5..1:5 mode=embedded literal="_" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
**a<http://foo.bar/?q=**>
.
Document scope=1:1..1:25 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:25 children=2
    ├── Text scope=1:1..1:3 literal="**a" children=0
    └── Link scope=1:4..1:25 destination="http://foo.bar/?q=**" title="" children=1
        └── Text scope=1:5..1:24 literal="http://foo.bar/?q=**" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
__a<http://foo.bar/?q=__>
.
Document scope=1:1..1:25 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:25 children=2
    ├── Text scope=1:1..1:3 literal="__a" children=0
    └── Link scope=1:4..1:25 destination="http://foo.bar/?q=__" title="" children=1
        └── Text scope=1:5..1:24 literal="http://foo.bar/?q=__" children=0
````````````````````````````````


<div class="extension">

## Strikethrough (extension)

GFM enables the `strikethrough` extension, where an additional emphasis type is
available.

Strikethrough text is any text wrapped in two tildes (`~`).

```````````````````````````````` example strikethrough
~~Hi~~ Hello, world!
.
Document scope=1:1..1:20 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:20 children=2
    ├── Strikethrough scope=1:1..1:6 children=1
    │   └── Text scope=1:3..1:4 literal="Hi" children=0
    └── Text scope=1:7..1:20 literal=" Hello, world!" children=0
````````````````````````````````

As with regular emphasis delimiters, a new paragraph will cause strikethrough
parsing to cease:

```````````````````````````````` example strikethrough
This ~~has a

new paragraph~~.
.
Document scope=1:1..3:16 children=2
├── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:12 children=1
│   └── Text scope=1:1..1:12 literal="This ~~has a" children=0
└── Paragraph scope=3:1..3:16 children=1
    └── Text scope=3:1..3:16 literal="new paragraph~~." children=0
````````````````````````````````

</div>

## Links

A link contains [link text] (the visible text), a [link destination]
(the URI that is the link destination), and optionally a [link title].
There are two basic kinds of links in Markdown.  In [inline links] the
destination and title are given immediately after the link text.  In
[reference links] the destination and title are defined elsewhere in
the document.

A [link text](@) consists of a sequence of zero or more
inline elements enclosed by square brackets (`[` and `]`).  The
following rules apply:

- Links may not contain other links, at any level of nesting. If
  multiple otherwise valid link definitions appear nested inside each
  other, the inner-most definition is used.

- Brackets are allowed in the [link text] only if (a) they
  are backslash-escaped or (b) they appear as a matched pair of brackets,
  with an open bracket `[`, a sequence of zero or more inlines, and
  a close bracket `]`.

- Backtick [code spans], [autolinks], and raw [HTML tags] bind more tightly
  than the brackets in link text.  Thus, for example,
  `` [foo`]` `` could not be a link text, since the second `]`
  is part of a code span.

- The brackets in link text bind more tightly than markers for
  [emphasis and strong emphasis]. Thus, for example, `*[foo*](url)` is a link.

A [link destination](@) consists of either

- a sequence of zero or more characters between an opening `<` and a
  closing `>` that contains no line breaks or unescaped
  `<` or `>` characters, or

- a nonempty sequence of characters that does not start with
  `<`, does not include ASCII space or control characters, and
  includes parentheses only if (a) they are backslash-escaped or
  (b) they are part of a balanced pair of unescaped parentheses.
  (Implementations may impose limits on parentheses nesting to
  avoid performance issues, but at least three levels of nesting
  should be supported.)

A [link title](@)  consists of either

- a sequence of zero or more characters between straight double-quote
  characters (`"`), including a `"` character only if it is
  backslash-escaped, or

- a sequence of zero or more characters between straight single-quote
  characters (`'`), including a `'` character only if it is
  backslash-escaped, or

- a sequence of zero or more characters between matching parentheses
  (`(...)`), including a `(` or `)` character only if it is
  backslash-escaped.

Although [link titles] may span multiple lines, they may not contain
a [blank line].

An [inline link](@) consists of a [link text] followed immediately
by a left parenthesis `(`, optional [whitespace], an optional
[link destination], an optional [link title] separated from the link
destination by [whitespace], optional [whitespace], and a right
parenthesis `)`. The link's text consists of the inlines contained
in the [link text] (excluding the enclosing square brackets).
The link's URI consists of the link destination, excluding enclosing
`<...>` if present, with backslash-escapes in effect as described
above.  The link's title consists of the link title, excluding its
enclosing delimiters, with backslash-escapes in effect as described
above.

Here is a simple inline link:

```````````````````````````````` example
[link](/uri "title")
.
Document scope=1:1..1:20 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:20 children=1
    └── Link scope=1:1..1:20 destination="/uri" title="title" children=1
        └── Text scope=1:2..1:5 literal="link" children=0
````````````````````````````````


The title may be omitted:

```````````````````````````````` example
[link](/uri)
.
Document scope=1:1..1:12 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:12 children=1
    └── Link scope=1:1..1:12 destination="/uri" title=null children=1
        └── Text scope=1:2..1:5 literal="link" children=0
````````````````````````````````


Both the title and the destination may be omitted:

```````````````````````````````` example
[link]()
.
Document scope=1:1..1:8 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:8 children=1
    └── Link scope=1:1..1:8 destination=null title=null children=1
        └── Text scope=1:2..1:5 literal="link" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
[link](<>)
.
Document scope=1:1..1:10 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:10 children=1
    └── Link scope=1:1..1:10 destination=null title=null children=1
        └── Text scope=1:2..1:5 literal="link" children=0
````````````````````````````````

The destination can only contain spaces if it is
enclosed in pointy brackets:

```````````````````````````````` example
[link](/my uri)
.
Document scope=1:1..1:15 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:15 children=1
    └── Text scope=1:1..1:15 literal="[link](/my uri)" children=0
````````````````````````````````

```````````````````````````````` example
[link](</my uri>)
.
Document scope=1:1..1:17 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:17 children=1
    └── Link scope=1:1..1:17 destination="/my uri" title=null children=1
        └── Text scope=1:2..1:5 literal="link" children=0
````````````````````````````````

The destination cannot contain line breaks,
even if enclosed in pointy brackets:

```````````````````````````````` example
[link](foo
bar)
.
Document scope=1:1..2:4 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..2:4 children=3
    ├── Text scope=1:1..1:10 literal="[link](foo" children=0
    ├── SoftBreak scope=0:0..0:0 children=0
    └── Text scope=2:1..2:4 literal="bar)" children=0
````````````````````````````````

```````````````````````````````` example
[link](<foo
bar>)
.
Document scope=1:1..2:5 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..2:5 children=3
    ├── Text scope=1:1..1:7 literal="[link](" children=0
    ├── HTML scope=1:8..1:16 literal="<foo\nbar>" children=0
    └── Text scope=1:17..1:17 literal=")" children=0
````````````````````````````````

The destination can contain `)` if it is enclosed
in pointy brackets:

```````````````````````````````` example
[a](<b)c>)
.
Document scope=1:1..1:10 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:10 children=1
    └── Link scope=1:1..1:10 destination="b)c" title=null children=1
        └── Text scope=1:2..1:2 literal="a" children=0
````````````````````````````````

Pointy brackets that enclose links must be unescaped:

```````````````````````````````` example
[link](<foo\>)
.
Document scope=1:1..1:14 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:14 children=1
    └── Text scope=1:1..1:14 literal="[link](<foo>)" children=0
````````````````````````````````

These are not links, because the opening pointy bracket
is not matched properly:

```````````````````````````````` example
[a](<b)c
[a](<b)c>
[a](<b>c)
.
Document scope=1:1..3:9 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..3:9 children=7
    ├── Text scope=1:1..1:8 literal="[a](<b)c" children=0
    ├── SoftBreak scope=0:0..0:0 children=0
    ├── Text scope=2:1..2:9 literal="[a](<b)c>" children=0
    ├── SoftBreak scope=0:0..0:0 children=0
    ├── Text scope=3:1..3:4 literal="[a](" children=0
    ├── HTML scope=3:5..3:7 literal="<b>" children=0
    └── Text scope=3:8..3:9 literal="c)" children=0
````````````````````````````````

Parentheses inside the link destination may be escaped:

```````````````````````````````` example
[link](\(foo\))
.
Document scope=1:1..1:15 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:15 children=1
    └── Link scope=1:1..1:15 destination="(foo)" title=null children=1
        └── Text scope=1:2..1:5 literal="link" children=0
````````````````````````````````

Any number of parentheses are allowed without escaping, as long as they are
balanced:

```````````````````````````````` example
[link](foo(and(bar)))
.
Document scope=1:1..1:21 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:21 children=1
    └── Link scope=1:1..1:21 destination="foo(and(bar))" title=null children=1
        └── Text scope=1:2..1:5 literal="link" children=0
````````````````````````````````

However, if you have unbalanced parentheses, you need to escape or use the
`<...>` form:

```````````````````````````````` example
[link](foo\(and\(bar\))
.
Document scope=1:1..1:23 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:23 children=1
    └── Link scope=1:1..1:23 destination="foo(and(bar)" title=null children=1
        └── Text scope=1:2..1:5 literal="link" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
[link](<foo(and(bar)>)
.
Document scope=1:1..1:22 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:22 children=1
    └── Link scope=1:1..1:22 destination="foo(and(bar)" title=null children=1
        └── Text scope=1:2..1:5 literal="link" children=0
````````````````````````````````


Parentheses and other symbols can also be escaped, as usual
in Markdown:

```````````````````````````````` example
[link](foo\)\:)
.
Document scope=1:1..1:15 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:15 children=1
    └── Link scope=1:1..1:15 destination="foo):" title=null children=1
        └── Text scope=1:2..1:5 literal="link" children=0
````````````````````````````````


A link can contain fragment identifiers and queries:

```````````````````````````````` example
[link](#fragment)

[link](http://example.com#fragment)

[link](http://example.com?foo=3#frag)
.
Document scope=1:1..5:37 children=3
├── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:17 children=1
│   └── Link scope=1:1..1:17 destination="#fragment" title=null children=1
│       └── Text scope=1:2..1:5 literal="link" children=0
├── Paragraph scope=3:1..3:35 children=1
│   └── Link scope=3:1..3:35 destination="http://example.com#fragment" title=null children=1
│       └── Text scope=3:2..3:5 literal="link" children=0
└── Paragraph scope=5:1..5:37 children=1
    └── Link scope=5:1..5:37 destination="http://example.com?foo=3#frag" title=null children=1
        └── Text scope=5:2..5:5 literal="link" children=0
````````````````````````````````


Note that a backslash before a non-escapable character is
just a backslash:

```````````````````````````````` example
[link](foo\bar)
.
Document scope=1:1..1:15 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:15 children=1
    └── Link scope=1:1..1:15 destination="foo\\bar" title=null children=1
        └── Text scope=1:2..1:5 literal="link" children=0
````````````````````````````````


URL-escaping should be left alone inside the destination, as all
URL-escaped characters are also valid URL characters. Entity and
numerical character references in the destination will be parsed
into the corresponding Unicode code points, as usual.  These may
be optionally URL-escaped when written as HTML, but this spec
does not enforce any particular policy for rendering URLs in
HTML or other formats.  Renderers may make different decisions
about how to escape or normalize URLs in the output.

```````````````````````````````` example
[link](foo%20b&auml;)
.
Document scope=1:1..1:21 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:21 children=1
    └── Link scope=1:1..1:21 destination="foo%20bä" title=null children=1
        └── Text scope=1:2..1:5 literal="link" children=0
````````````````````````````````


Note that, because titles can often be parsed as destinations,
if you try to omit the destination and keep the title, you'll
get unexpected results:

```````````````````````````````` example
[link]("title")
.
Document scope=1:1..1:15 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:15 children=1
    └── Link scope=1:1..1:15 destination="\"title\"" title=null children=1
        └── Text scope=1:2..1:5 literal="link" children=0
````````````````````````````````


Titles may be in single quotes, double quotes, or parentheses:

```````````````````````````````` example
[link](/url "title")
[link](/url 'title')
[link](/url (title))
.
Document scope=1:1..3:20 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..3:20 children=5
    ├── Link scope=1:1..1:20 destination="/url" title="title" children=1
    │   └── Text scope=1:2..1:5 literal="link" children=0
    ├── SoftBreak scope=0:0..0:0 children=0
    ├── Link scope=2:1..2:20 destination="/url" title="title" children=1
    │   └── Text scope=2:2..2:5 literal="link" children=0
    ├── SoftBreak scope=0:0..0:0 children=0
    └── Link scope=3:1..3:20 destination="/url" title="title" children=1
        └── Text scope=3:2..3:5 literal="link" children=0
````````````````````````````````


Backslash escapes and entity and numeric character references
may be used in titles:

```````````````````````````````` example
[link](/url "title \"&quot;")
.
Document scope=1:1..1:29 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:29 children=1
    └── Link scope=1:1..1:29 destination="/url" title="title \"\"" children=1
        └── Text scope=1:2..1:5 literal="link" children=0
````````````````````````````````


Titles must be separated from the link using a [whitespace].
Other [Unicode whitespace] like non-breaking space doesn't work.

```````````````````````````````` example
[link](/url "title")
.
Document scope=1:1..1:21 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:21 children=1
    └── Link scope=1:1..1:21 destination="/url \"title\"" title=null children=1
        └── Text scope=1:2..1:5 literal="link" children=0
````````````````````````````````


Nested balanced quotes are not allowed without escaping:

```````````````````````````````` example
[link](/url "title "and" title")
.
Document scope=1:1..1:32 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:32 children=1
    └── Text scope=1:1..1:32 literal="[link](/url \"title \"and\" title\")" children=0
````````````````````````````````


But it is easy to work around this by using a different quote type:

```````````````````````````````` example
[link](/url 'title "and" title')
.
Document scope=1:1..1:32 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:32 children=1
    └── Link scope=1:1..1:32 destination="/url" title="title \"and\" title" children=1
        └── Text scope=1:2..1:5 literal="link" children=0
````````````````````````````````


(Note:  `Markdown.pl` did allow double quotes inside a double-quoted
title, and its test suite included a test demonstrating this.
But it is hard to see a good rationale for the extra complexity this
brings, since there are already many ways---backslash escaping,
entity and numeric character references, or using a different
quote type for the enclosing title---to write titles containing
double quotes.  `Markdown.pl`'s handling of titles has a number
of other strange features.  For example, it allows single-quoted
titles in inline links, but not reference links.  And, in
reference links but not inline links, it allows a title to begin
with `"` and end with `)`.  `Markdown.pl` 1.0.1 even allows
titles with no closing quotation mark, though 1.0.2b8 does not.
It seems preferable to adopt a simple, rational rule that works
the same way in inline links and link reference definitions.)

[Whitespace] is allowed around the destination and title:

```````````````````````````````` example
[link](   /uri
  "title"  )
.
Document scope=1:1..2:12 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..2:12 children=1
    └── Link scope=1:1..1:25 destination="/uri" title="title" children=1
        └── Text scope=1:2..1:5 literal="link" children=0
````````````````````````````````


But it is not allowed between the link text and the
following parenthesis:

```````````````````````````````` example
[link] (/uri)
.
Document scope=1:1..1:13 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:13 children=1
    └── Text scope=1:1..1:13 literal="[link] (/uri)" children=0
````````````````````````````````


The link text may contain balanced brackets, but not unbalanced ones,
unless they are escaped:

```````````````````````````````` example
[link [foo [bar]]](/uri)
.
Document scope=1:1..1:24 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:24 children=1
    └── Link scope=1:1..1:24 destination="/uri" title=null children=1
        └── Text scope=1:2..1:17 literal="link [foo [bar]]" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
[link] bar](/uri)
.
Document scope=1:1..1:17 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:17 children=1
    └── Text scope=1:1..1:17 literal="[link] bar](/uri)" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
[link [bar](/uri)
.
Document scope=1:1..1:17 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:17 children=2
    ├── Text scope=1:1..1:6 literal="[link " children=0
    └── Link scope=1:7..1:17 destination="/uri" title=null children=1
        └── Text scope=1:8..1:10 literal="bar" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
[link \[bar](/uri)
.
Document scope=1:1..1:18 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:18 children=1
    └── Link scope=1:1..1:18 destination="/uri" title=null children=1
        └── Text scope=1:2..1:11 literal="link [bar" children=0
````````````````````````````````


The link text may contain inline content:

```````````````````````````````` example
[link *foo **bar** `#`*](/uri)
.
Document scope=1:1..1:30 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:30 children=1
    └── Link scope=1:1..1:30 destination="/uri" title=null children=2
        ├── Text scope=1:2..1:6 literal="link " children=0
        └── Emphasis scope=1:7..1:23 children=4
            ├── Text scope=1:8..1:11 literal="foo " children=0
            ├── Strong scope=1:12..1:18 children=1
            │   └── Text scope=1:14..1:16 literal="bar" children=0
            ├── Text scope=1:19..1:19 literal=" " children=0
            └── Code scope=1:21..1:21 mode=embedded literal="#" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
[![moon](moon.jpg)](/uri)
.
Document scope=1:1..1:25 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:25 children=1
    └── Link scope=1:1..1:25 destination="/uri" title=null children=1
        └── Image scope=1:2..1:18 source="moon.jpg" title=null children=1
            └── Text scope=1:4..1:7 literal="moon" children=0
````````````````````````````````


However, links may not contain other links, at any level of nesting.

```````````````````````````````` example
[foo [bar](/uri)](/uri)
.
Document scope=1:1..1:23 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:23 children=3
    ├── Text scope=1:1..1:5 literal="[foo " children=0
    ├── Link scope=1:6..1:16 destination="/uri" title=null children=1
    │   └── Text scope=1:7..1:9 literal="bar" children=0
    └── Text scope=1:17..1:23 literal="](/uri)" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
[foo *[bar [baz](/uri)](/uri)*](/uri)
.
Document scope=1:1..1:37 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:37 children=3
    ├── Text scope=1:1..1:5 literal="[foo " children=0
    ├── Emphasis scope=1:6..1:30 children=3
    │   ├── Text scope=1:7..1:11 literal="[bar " children=0
    │   ├── Link scope=1:12..1:22 destination="/uri" title=null children=1
    │   │   └── Text scope=1:13..1:15 literal="baz" children=0
    │   └── Text scope=1:23..1:29 literal="](/uri)" children=0
    └── Text scope=1:31..1:37 literal="](/uri)" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
![[[foo](uri1)](uri2)](uri3)
.
Document scope=1:1..1:28 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:28 children=1
    └── Image scope=1:1..1:28 source="uri3" title=null children=3
        ├── Text scope=1:3..1:3 literal="[" children=0
        ├── Link scope=1:4..1:14 destination="uri1" title=null children=1
        │   └── Text scope=1:5..1:7 literal="foo" children=0
        └── Text scope=1:15..1:21 literal="](uri2)" children=0
````````````````````````````````


These cases illustrate the precedence of link text grouping over
emphasis grouping:

```````````````````````````````` example
*[foo*](/uri)
.
Document scope=1:1..1:13 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:13 children=2
    ├── Text scope=1:1..1:1 literal="*" children=0
    └── Link scope=1:2..1:13 destination="/uri" title=null children=1
        └── Text scope=1:3..1:6 literal="foo*" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
[foo *bar](baz*)
.
Document scope=1:1..1:16 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:16 children=1
    └── Link scope=1:1..1:16 destination="baz*" title=null children=1
        └── Text scope=1:2..1:9 literal="foo *bar" children=0
````````````````````````````````


Note that brackets that *aren't* part of links do not take
precedence:

```````````````````````````````` example
*foo [bar* baz]
.
Document scope=1:1..1:15 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:15 children=2
    ├── Emphasis scope=1:1..1:10 children=1
    │   └── Text scope=1:2..1:9 literal="foo [bar" children=0
    └── Text scope=1:11..1:15 literal=" baz]" children=0
````````````````````````````````


These cases illustrate the precedence of HTML tags, code spans,
and autolinks over link grouping:

```````````````````````````````` example
[foo <bar attr="](baz)">
.
Document scope=1:1..1:24 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:24 children=2
    ├── Text scope=1:1..1:5 literal="[foo " children=0
    └── HTML scope=1:6..1:24 literal="<bar attr=\"](baz)\">" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
[foo`](/uri)`
.
Document scope=1:1..1:13 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:13 children=2
    ├── Text scope=1:1..1:4 literal="[foo" children=0
    └── Code scope=1:6..1:12 mode=embedded literal="](/uri)" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
[foo<http://example.com/?search=](uri)>
.
Document scope=1:1..1:39 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:39 children=2
    ├── Text scope=1:1..1:4 literal="[foo" children=0
    └── Link scope=1:5..1:39 destination="http://example.com/?search=](uri)" title="" children=1
        └── Text scope=1:6..1:38 literal="http://example.com/?search=](uri)" children=0
````````````````````````````````


There are three kinds of [reference link](@)s:
[full](#full-reference-link), [collapsed](#collapsed-reference-link),
and [shortcut](#shortcut-reference-link).

A [full reference link](@)
consists of a [link text] immediately followed by a [link label]
that [matches] a [link reference definition] elsewhere in the document.

A [link label](@)  begins with a left bracket (`[`) and ends
with the first right bracket (`]`) that is not backslash-escaped.
Between these brackets there must be at least one [non-whitespace character].
Unescaped square bracket characters are not allowed inside the
opening and closing square brackets of [link labels].  A link
label can have at most 999 characters inside the square
brackets.

One label [matches](@)
another just in case their normalized forms are equal.  To normalize a
label, strip off the opening and closing brackets,
perform the *Unicode case fold*, strip leading and trailing
[whitespace] and collapse consecutive internal
[whitespace] to a single space.  If there are multiple
matching reference link definitions, the one that comes first in the
document is used.  (It is desirable in such cases to emit a warning.)

The contents of the first link label are parsed as inlines, which are
used as the link's text.  The link's URI and title are provided by the
matching [link reference definition].

Here is a simple example:

```````````````````````````````` example
[foo][bar]

[bar]: /url "title"
.
Document scope=1:1..3:19 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:10 children=1
    └── Link scope=1:1..1:10 destination="/url" title="title" children=1
        └── Text scope=1:2..1:4 literal="foo" children=0
````````````````````````````````


The rules for the [link text] are the same as with
[inline links].  Thus:

The link text may contain balanced brackets, but not unbalanced ones,
unless they are escaped:

```````````````````````````````` example
[link [foo [bar]]][ref]

[ref]: /uri
.
Document scope=1:1..3:11 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:23 children=1
    └── Link scope=1:1..1:23 destination="/uri" title="" children=1
        └── Text scope=1:2..1:17 literal="link [foo [bar]]" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
[link \[bar][ref]

[ref]: /uri
.
Document scope=1:1..3:11 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:17 children=1
    └── Link scope=1:1..1:17 destination="/uri" title="" children=1
        └── Text scope=1:2..1:11 literal="link [bar" children=0
````````````````````````````````


The link text may contain inline content:

```````````````````````````````` example
[link *foo **bar** `#`*][ref]

[ref]: /uri
.
Document scope=1:1..3:11 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:29 children=1
    └── Link scope=1:1..1:29 destination="/uri" title="" children=2
        ├── Text scope=1:2..1:6 literal="link " children=0
        └── Emphasis scope=1:7..1:23 children=4
            ├── Text scope=1:8..1:11 literal="foo " children=0
            ├── Strong scope=1:12..1:18 children=1
            │   └── Text scope=1:14..1:16 literal="bar" children=0
            ├── Text scope=1:19..1:19 literal=" " children=0
            └── Code scope=1:21..1:21 mode=embedded literal="#" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
[![moon](moon.jpg)][ref]

[ref]: /uri
.
Document scope=1:1..3:11 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:24 children=1
    └── Link scope=1:1..1:24 destination="/uri" title="" children=1
        └── Image scope=1:2..1:18 source="moon.jpg" title=null children=1
            └── Text scope=1:4..1:7 literal="moon" children=0
````````````````````````````````


However, links may not contain other links, at any level of nesting.

```````````````````````````````` example
[foo [bar](/uri)][ref]

[ref]: /uri
.
Document scope=1:1..3:11 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:22 children=4
    ├── Text scope=1:1..1:5 literal="[foo " children=0
    ├── Link scope=1:6..1:16 destination="/uri" title=null children=1
    │   └── Text scope=1:7..1:9 literal="bar" children=0
    ├── Text scope=1:17..1:17 literal="]" children=0
    └── Link scope=1:18..1:22 destination="/uri" title="" children=1
        └── Text scope=1:19..1:21 literal="ref" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
[foo *bar [baz][ref]*][ref]

[ref]: /uri
.
Document scope=1:1..3:11 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:27 children=4
    ├── Text scope=1:1..1:5 literal="[foo " children=0
    ├── Emphasis scope=1:6..1:21 children=2
    │   ├── Text scope=1:7..1:10 literal="bar " children=0
    │   └── Link scope=1:11..1:20 destination="/uri" title="" children=1
    │       └── Text scope=1:12..1:14 literal="baz" children=0
    ├── Text scope=1:22..1:22 literal="]" children=0
    └── Link scope=1:23..1:27 destination="/uri" title="" children=1
        └── Text scope=1:24..1:26 literal="ref" children=0
````````````````````````````````


(In the examples above, we have two [shortcut reference links]
instead of one [full reference link].)

The following cases illustrate the precedence of link text grouping over
emphasis grouping:

```````````````````````````````` example
*[foo*][ref]

[ref]: /uri
.
Document scope=1:1..3:11 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:12 children=2
    ├── Text scope=1:1..1:1 literal="*" children=0
    └── Link scope=1:2..1:12 destination="/uri" title="" children=1
        └── Text scope=1:3..1:6 literal="foo*" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
[foo *bar][ref]

[ref]: /uri
.
Document scope=1:1..3:11 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:15 children=1
    └── Link scope=1:1..1:15 destination="/uri" title="" children=1
        └── Text scope=1:2..1:9 literal="foo *bar" children=0
````````````````````````````````


These cases illustrate the precedence of HTML tags, code spans,
and autolinks over link grouping:

```````````````````````````````` example
[foo <bar attr="][ref]">

[ref]: /uri
.
Document scope=1:1..3:11 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:24 children=2
    ├── Text scope=1:1..1:5 literal="[foo " children=0
    └── HTML scope=1:6..1:24 literal="<bar attr=\"][ref]\">" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
[foo`][ref]`

[ref]: /uri
.
Document scope=1:1..3:11 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:12 children=2
    ├── Text scope=1:1..1:4 literal="[foo" children=0
    └── Code scope=1:6..1:11 mode=embedded literal="][ref]" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
[foo<http://example.com/?search=][ref]>

[ref]: /uri
.
Document scope=1:1..3:11 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:39 children=2
    ├── Text scope=1:1..1:4 literal="[foo" children=0
    └── Link scope=1:5..1:39 destination="http://example.com/?search=][ref]" title="" children=1
        └── Text scope=1:6..1:38 literal="http://example.com/?search=][ref]" children=0
````````````````````````````````


Matching is case-insensitive:

```````````````````````````````` example
[foo][BaR]

[bar]: /url "title"
.
Document scope=1:1..3:19 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:10 children=1
    └── Link scope=1:1..1:10 destination="/url" title="title" children=1
        └── Text scope=1:2..1:4 literal="foo" children=0
````````````````````````````````


Unicode case fold is used:

```````````````````````````````` example
[Толпой][Толпой] is a Russian word.

[ТОЛПОЙ]: /url
.
Document scope=1:1..3:20 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:47 children=2
    ├── Link scope=1:1..1:28 destination="/url" title="" children=1
    │   └── Text scope=1:2..1:13 literal="Толпой" children=0
    └── Text scope=1:29..1:47 literal=" is a Russian word." children=0
````````````````````````````````


Consecutive internal [whitespace] is treated as one space for
purposes of determining matching:

```````````````````````````````` example
[Foo
  bar]: /url

[Baz][Foo bar]
.
Document scope=1:1..4:14 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=4:1..4:14 children=1
    └── Link scope=4:1..4:14 destination="/url" title="" children=1
        └── Text scope=4:2..4:4 literal="Baz" children=0
````````````````````````````````


No [whitespace] is allowed between the [link text] and the
[link label]:

```````````````````````````````` example
[foo] [bar]

[bar]: /url "title"
.
Document scope=1:1..3:19 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:11 children=2
    ├── Text scope=1:1..1:6 literal="[foo] " children=0
    └── Link scope=1:7..1:11 destination="/url" title="title" children=1
        └── Text scope=1:8..1:10 literal="bar" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
[foo]
[bar]

[bar]: /url "title"
.
Document scope=1:1..4:19 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..2:5 children=3
    ├── Text scope=1:1..1:5 literal="[foo]" children=0
    ├── SoftBreak scope=0:0..0:0 children=0
    └── Link scope=2:1..2:5 destination="/url" title="title" children=1
        └── Text scope=2:2..2:4 literal="bar" children=0
````````````````````````````````


This is a departure from John Gruber's original Markdown syntax
description, which explicitly allows whitespace between the link
text and the link label.  It brings reference links in line with
[inline links], which (according to both original Markdown and
this spec) cannot have whitespace after the link text.  More
importantly, it prevents inadvertent capture of consecutive
[shortcut reference links]. If whitespace is allowed between the
link text and the link label, then in the following we will have
a single reference link, not two shortcut reference links, as
intended:

``` markdown
[foo]
[bar]

[foo]: /url1
[bar]: /url2
```

(Note that [shortcut reference links] were introduced by Gruber
himself in a beta version of `Markdown.pl`, but never included
in the official syntax description.  Without shortcut reference
links, it is harmless to allow space between the link text and
link label; but once shortcut references are introduced, it is
too dangerous to allow this, as it frequently leads to
unintended results.)

When there are multiple matching [link reference definitions],
the first is used:

```````````````````````````````` example
[foo]: /url1

[foo]: /url2

[bar][foo]
.
Document scope=1:1..5:10 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=5:1..5:10 children=1
    └── Link scope=5:1..5:10 destination="/url1" title="" children=1
        └── Text scope=5:2..5:4 literal="bar" children=0
````````````````````````````````


Note that matching is performed on normalized strings, not parsed
inline content.  So the following does not match, even though the
labels define equivalent inline content:

```````````````````````````````` example
[bar][foo\!]

[foo!]: /url
.
Document scope=1:1..3:12 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:12 children=1
    └── Text scope=1:1..1:12 literal="[bar][foo!]" children=0
````````````````````````````````


[Link labels] cannot contain brackets, unless they are
backslash-escaped:

```````````````````````````````` example
[foo][ref[]

[ref[]: /uri
.
Document scope=1:1..3:12 children=2
├── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:11 children=1
│   └── Text scope=1:1..1:11 literal="[foo][ref[]" children=0
└── Paragraph scope=3:1..3:12 children=1
    └── Text scope=3:1..3:12 literal="[ref[]: /uri" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
[foo][ref[bar]]

[ref[bar]]: /uri
.
Document scope=1:1..3:16 children=2
├── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:15 children=1
│   └── Text scope=1:1..1:15 literal="[foo][ref[bar]]" children=0
└── Paragraph scope=3:1..3:16 children=1
    └── Text scope=3:1..3:16 literal="[ref[bar]]: /uri" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
[[[foo]]]

[[[foo]]]: /url
.
Document scope=1:1..3:15 children=2
├── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:9 children=1
│   └── Text scope=1:1..1:9 literal="[[[foo]]]" children=0
└── Paragraph scope=3:1..3:15 children=1
    └── Text scope=3:1..3:15 literal="[[[foo]]]: /url" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
[foo][ref\[]

[ref\[]: /uri
.
Document scope=1:1..3:13 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:12 children=1
    └── Link scope=1:1..1:12 destination="/uri" title="" children=1
        └── Text scope=1:2..1:4 literal="foo" children=0
````````````````````````````````


Note that in this example `]` is not backslash-escaped:

```````````````````````````````` example
[bar\\]: /uri

[bar\\]
.
Document scope=1:1..3:7 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=3:1..3:7 children=1
    └── Link scope=3:1..3:7 destination="/uri" title="" children=1
        └── Text scope=3:2..3:6 literal="bar\\" children=0
````````````````````````````````


A [link label] must contain at least one [non-whitespace character]:

```````````````````````````````` example
[]

[]: /uri
.
Document scope=1:1..3:8 children=2
├── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:2 children=1
│   └── Text scope=1:1..1:2 literal="[]" children=0
└── Paragraph scope=3:1..3:8 children=1
    └── Text scope=3:1..3:8 literal="[]: /uri" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
[
 ]

[
 ]: /uri
.
Document scope=1:1..5:8 children=2
├── Paragraph scope=1:1..2:2 children=3
│   ├── Text scope=1:1..1:1 literal="[" children=0
│   ├── SoftBreak scope=0:0..0:0 children=0
│   └── Text scope=2:1..2:1 literal="]" children=0
└── Paragraph scope=4:1..5:8 children=3
    ├── Text scope=4:1..4:1 literal="[" children=0
    ├── SoftBreak scope=0:0..0:0 children=0
    └── Text scope=5:1..5:7 literal="]: /uri" children=0
````````````````````````````````


A [collapsed reference link](@)
consists of a [link label] that [matches] a
[link reference definition] elsewhere in the
document, followed by the string `[]`.
The contents of the first link label are parsed as inlines,
which are used as the link's text.  The link's URI and title are
provided by the matching reference link definition.  Thus,
`[foo][]` is equivalent to `[foo][foo]`.

```````````````````````````````` example
[foo][]

[foo]: /url "title"
.
Document scope=1:1..3:19 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:7 children=1
    └── Link scope=1:1..1:7 destination="/url" title="title" children=1
        └── Text scope=1:2..1:4 literal="foo" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
[*foo* bar][]

[*foo* bar]: /url "title"
.
Document scope=1:1..3:25 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:13 children=1
    └── Link scope=1:1..1:13 destination="/url" title="title" children=2
        ├── Emphasis scope=1:2..1:6 children=1
        │   └── Text scope=1:3..1:5 literal="foo" children=0
        └── Text scope=1:7..1:10 literal=" bar" children=0
````````````````````````````````


The link labels are case-insensitive:

```````````````````````````````` example
[Foo][]

[foo]: /url "title"
.
Document scope=1:1..3:19 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:7 children=1
    └── Link scope=1:1..1:7 destination="/url" title="title" children=1
        └── Text scope=1:2..1:4 literal="Foo" children=0
````````````````````````````````



As with full reference links, [whitespace] is not
allowed between the two sets of brackets:

```````````````````````````````` example
[foo] 
[]

[foo]: /url "title"
.
Document scope=1:1..4:19 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..2:2 children=4
    ├── Link scope=1:1..1:5 destination="/url" title="title" children=1
    │   └── Text scope=1:2..1:4 literal="foo" children=0
    ├── Text scope=1:6..1:6 literal="" children=0
    ├── SoftBreak scope=0:0..0:0 children=0
    └── Text scope=2:1..2:2 literal="[]" children=0
````````````````````````````````


A [shortcut reference link](@)
consists of a [link label] that [matches] a
[link reference definition] elsewhere in the
document and is not followed by `[]` or a link label.
The contents of the first link label are parsed as inlines,
which are used as the link's text.  The link's URI and title
are provided by the matching link reference definition.
Thus, `[foo]` is equivalent to `[foo][]`.

```````````````````````````````` example
[foo]

[foo]: /url "title"
.
Document scope=1:1..3:19 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:5 children=1
    └── Link scope=1:1..1:5 destination="/url" title="title" children=1
        └── Text scope=1:2..1:4 literal="foo" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
[*foo* bar]

[*foo* bar]: /url "title"
.
Document scope=1:1..3:25 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:11 children=1
    └── Link scope=1:1..1:11 destination="/url" title="title" children=2
        ├── Emphasis scope=1:2..1:6 children=1
        │   └── Text scope=1:3..1:5 literal="foo" children=0
        └── Text scope=1:7..1:10 literal=" bar" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
[[*foo* bar]]

[*foo* bar]: /url "title"
.
Document scope=1:1..3:25 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:13 children=3
    ├── Text scope=1:1..1:1 literal="[" children=0
    ├── Link scope=1:2..1:12 destination="/url" title="title" children=2
    │   ├── Emphasis scope=1:3..1:7 children=1
    │   │   └── Text scope=1:4..1:6 literal="foo" children=0
    │   └── Text scope=1:8..1:11 literal=" bar" children=0
    └── Text scope=1:13..1:13 literal="]" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
[[bar [foo]

[foo]: /url
.
Document scope=1:1..3:11 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:11 children=2
    ├── Text scope=1:1..1:6 literal="[[bar " children=0
    └── Link scope=1:7..1:11 destination="/url" title="" children=1
        └── Text scope=1:8..1:10 literal="foo" children=0
````````````````````````````````


The link labels are case-insensitive:

```````````````````````````````` example
[Foo]

[foo]: /url "title"
.
Document scope=1:1..3:19 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:5 children=1
    └── Link scope=1:1..1:5 destination="/url" title="title" children=1
        └── Text scope=1:2..1:4 literal="Foo" children=0
````````````````````````````````


A space after the link text should be preserved:

```````````````````````````````` example
[foo] bar

[foo]: /url
.
Document scope=1:1..3:11 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:9 children=2
    ├── Link scope=1:1..1:5 destination="/url" title="" children=1
    │   └── Text scope=1:2..1:4 literal="foo" children=0
    └── Text scope=1:6..1:9 literal=" bar" children=0
````````````````````````````````


If you just want bracketed text, you can backslash-escape the
opening bracket to avoid links:

```````````````````````````````` example
\[foo]

[foo]: /url "title"
.
Document scope=1:1..3:19 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:6 children=1
    └── Text scope=1:1..1:6 literal="[foo]" children=0
````````````````````````````````


Note that this is a link, because a link label ends with the first
following closing bracket:

```````````````````````````````` example
[foo*]: /url

*[foo*]
.
Document scope=1:1..3:7 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=3:1..3:7 children=2
    ├── Text scope=3:1..3:1 literal="*" children=0
    └── Link scope=3:2..3:7 destination="/url" title="" children=1
        └── Text scope=3:3..3:6 literal="foo*" children=0
````````````````````````````````


Full and compact references take precedence over shortcut
references:

```````````````````````````````` example
[foo][bar]

[foo]: /url1
[bar]: /url2
.
Document scope=1:1..4:12 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:10 children=1
    └── Link scope=1:1..1:10 destination="/url2" title="" children=1
        └── Text scope=1:2..1:4 literal="foo" children=0
````````````````````````````````

```````````````````````````````` example
[foo][]

[foo]: /url1
.
Document scope=1:1..3:12 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:7 children=1
    └── Link scope=1:1..1:7 destination="/url1" title="" children=1
        └── Text scope=1:2..1:4 literal="foo" children=0
````````````````````````````````

Inline links also take precedence:

```````````````````````````````` example
[foo]()

[foo]: /url1
.
Document scope=1:1..3:12 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:7 children=1
    └── Link scope=1:1..1:7 destination=null title=null children=1
        └── Text scope=1:2..1:4 literal="foo" children=0
````````````````````````````````

```````````````````````````````` example
[foo](not a link)

[foo]: /url1
.
Document scope=1:1..3:12 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:17 children=2
    ├── Link scope=1:1..1:5 destination="/url1" title="" children=1
    │   └── Text scope=1:2..1:4 literal="foo" children=0
    └── Text scope=1:6..1:17 literal="(not a link)" children=0
````````````````````````````````

In the following case `[bar][baz]` is parsed as a reference,
`[foo]` as normal text:

```````````````````````````````` example
[foo][bar][baz]

[baz]: /url
.
Document scope=1:1..3:11 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:15 children=2
    ├── Text scope=1:1..1:5 literal="[foo]" children=0
    └── Link scope=1:6..1:15 destination="/url" title="" children=1
        └── Text scope=1:7..1:9 literal="bar" children=0
````````````````````````````````


Here, though, `[foo][bar]` is parsed as a reference, since
`[bar]` is defined:

```````````````````````````````` example
[foo][bar][baz]

[baz]: /url1
[bar]: /url2
.
Document scope=1:1..4:12 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:15 children=2
    ├── Link scope=1:1..1:10 destination="/url2" title="" children=1
    │   └── Text scope=1:2..1:4 literal="foo" children=0
    └── Link scope=1:11..1:15 destination="/url1" title="" children=1
        └── Text scope=1:12..1:14 literal="baz" children=0
````````````````````````````````


Here `[foo]` is not parsed as a shortcut reference, because it
is followed by a link label (even though `[bar]` is not defined):

```````````````````````````````` example
[foo][bar][baz]

[baz]: /url1
[foo]: /url2
.
Document scope=1:1..4:12 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:15 children=2
    ├── Text scope=1:1..1:5 literal="[foo]" children=0
    └── Link scope=1:6..1:15 destination="/url1" title="" children=1
        └── Text scope=1:7..1:9 literal="bar" children=0
````````````````````````````````



## Images

Syntax for images is like the syntax for links, with one
difference. Instead of [link text], we have an
[image description](@).  The rules for this are the
same as for [link text], except that (a) an
image description starts with `![` rather than `[`, and
(b) an image description may contain links.
An image description has inline elements
as its contents.  When an image is rendered to HTML,
this is standardly used as the image's `alt` attribute.

```````````````````````````````` example
![foo](/url "title")
.
Document scope=1:1..1:20 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:20 children=1
    └── Image scope=1:1..1:20 source="/url" title="title" children=1
        └── Text scope=1:3..1:5 literal="foo" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
![foo *bar*]

[foo *bar*]: train.jpg "train & tracks"
.
Document scope=1:1..3:39 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:12 children=1
    └── Image scope=1:1..1:12 source="train.jpg" title="train & tracks" children=2
        ├── Text scope=1:3..1:6 literal="foo " children=0
        └── Emphasis scope=1:7..1:11 children=1
            └── Text scope=1:8..1:10 literal="bar" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
![foo ![bar](/url)](/url2)
.
Document scope=1:1..1:26 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:26 children=1
    └── Image scope=1:1..1:26 source="/url2" title=null children=2
        ├── Text scope=1:3..1:6 literal="foo " children=0
        └── Image scope=1:7..1:18 source="/url" title=null children=1
            └── Text scope=1:9..1:11 literal="bar" children=0
````````````````````````````````


```````````````````````````````` example
![foo [bar](/url)](/url2)
.
Document scope=1:1..1:25 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:25 children=1
    └── Image scope=1:1..1:25 source="/url2" title=null children=2
        ├── Text scope=1:3..1:6 literal="foo " children=0
        └── Link scope=1:7..1:17 destination="/url" title=null children=1
            └── Text scope=1:8..1:10 literal="bar" children=0
````````````````````````````````


Though this spec is concerned with parsing, not rendering, it is
recommended that in rendering to HTML, only the plain string content
of the [image description] be used.  Note that in
